STEEL AUTHORITY OF INDIA LIMITED
IISCO STEEL PLANT (ISP), BURNPUR
TECHNICAL SPECIFICATION
FOR
CIVIL AND STRCTURAL WORKS
INCLUDING EQUIPMENT ERECTION WORKS
FOR
HOT STRIP MILL
(PACKAGE- HSM-04)
MECON LIMITED
RANCHI – 834002
PART – C
TECHNICAL SPECIFICATION
OF HSM SHOP
(ERECTION WORKS)
INTRODUCTION

The 4.0 MTPA Hot Strip Mill (HSM) project shall be executed through four (4) packages i.e Process Technology package (HSM-1), Balance of Plant (BOP) package (HSM-2), EOT Cranes package (HSM-3) and Civil, Structural & Erection package (HSM-4).

This Technical Specification (TS) for Package HSM-4 has been prepared for the Civil, Structural and Erection Work of 4.0 MTPA Hot Strip Mill for executing civil and structural works for the entire HSM complex i.e. for the plant and facilities under the scope of package HSM-1, HSM-2 and HSM-3 and erection works for plant and equipment to be supplied under scope of Package HSM-1 & Package HSM-3. Scope of work has been covered in the subsequent chapters of this Technical Specification.
This specification deals in three (3) parts for the Civil, Steel Structure and Equipment Erection works:
Part A : Civil work
Part B : Steel Structures Work
Part C :Equipment Erection Work

The Part-C of this Technical Specificationhas been prepared for the erection, testing and commissioning of Plant and Equipment to be supplied under scope of Package HSM-1 & Package HSM-3for HSM Complex.

The erection works of plant and equipment covered under scope of Package HSM4 shall be executed in a very tight time schedule matching with the overall implementation schedule of various units of the integrated steel Plant.
**End of Clauses**

records.text: This technical specification intends for the equipment handling, erection, assistance in testing & commissioning of plant and equipment supplied by respective equipment supplier for Hot Strip Mill complex. The plant and equipment to be supplied under Package HSM-1 (Process Technology supplier package) broadly includes mechanical, electrical, technological structures, lubrication and hydraulic equipment, instrumentation, piping etc. and Package HSM-3 includes EOT cranes and their hoists.

records.text: In order to make the accountable against the issued material such as pipes, steel, etc. following methodology has been considered or shall be provided by the PT supplier. Any excess beyond the permissible wastage limit shall be recovered from the at 1.5 times the market rates.
The total quantity of pipes / steel issued shall be accounted for and the maximum permissible wastage on the quantity of pipes incorporated in the work shall be as follows:
Invisible/irrecoverable wastage will be considered upto 2% (two percent)
Recoverable wastage in the form of off-cuts/scrap will be considered upto 3% (three percent).

The difference between the total quantity of pipes issued by the Employer and the quantity of pipes accounted (i.e., the quantity of pipes consumed in the work plus the quantity of surplus material returned in the form of full-length pipes), shall be within permissible wastage. Any excess difference shall be treated as the quantity of unaccounted pipes. Unused accessories (difference between the total quantity of accessories issued by the Employer and the quantity of accessories accounted for the work) shall be returned to Employer’s stores at s’ cost, failing which recovery shall be made for such unaccounted pipes & accessories.

Certification of quantity of work carried out by the shall be done by the Purchaser/ Purchaser’s representative. The work quantity shall be certified on the basis of joint measurements taken by and certified by Client/ Consultant/ Supplier of equipment. Measurement shall be based on Approved drawings, specification and manuals to the extent that the work conforms to the drawings and details are adequate. For final reconciliation, the weight of equipment will be based on packing list weight.
Payments shall be released against the invoices of based on quantities certified and unit rate of respective item as per BOQ.
There shall be no compensation by the Purchaser for any claim of idle labour and handling/ erection equipment on any account.

records.text: It will be the principal function of the site in-charge of the to organize a group with qualified engineers primarily with a view to ensure the safety of all concerned people during the erection and subsequently to advise Purchaser on matters of industrial safety.
Broad functions of this group are enumerated below: -
Grading of work situations with regard to their propensity for safety
Identifying areas of work needing safety measures to be undertaken.
Devise appropriate safety methodologies for follow-up by all concerned with regard to national codes / statutory regulations.
Work out provision of minimum quantum of safety appliances as
Applicable to a variety of work situations for follow-up.

Periodically check the working conditions in various erection zones with regard to noise level, ventilation provisions, level of environmental contamination, fire hazards, concentration of heavy erection equipment in work areas, etc. and to recommend/enforce suitable safety measures.
All safety measures as required to be adopted as per the statutory regulations and the safety rules of the plant shall be strictly followed by the during the execution of the Contract.
All safety rules and statutory regulations may undergo changes with time, to follow the latest as per safety rules and statutory regulations.
The shall take all necessary precautions against fire hazards and shall provide suitable fire extinguishers at appropriate places at the work sites and site storages.
To ensure that the agencies are aware of the appropriate safety provisions to be made by them.

records.section: 6. GENERAL DESCRIPTION OF HOT STRIP MILL COMPLEX

records.text: The proposed Hot Strip Mill will be designed for a rated production capacity of 4.0 MTPA. A Cut-to-length line with a capacity of 240,000 TPA shall also be installed for processing HR Coils into the HR Plates / Sheets in one of the coil storage bay as per the layout.

Slabs from continuous slab caster will be stored in piles in the slab yard. Slabs from slab yard will be kept onto the furnace charging roller table with the help of overhead cranes. Then, Slab charging system will charge slabs into the walking beam type reheating furnace. The design shall also include the provision for hot charging of the slabs directly from caster to the reheating furnace.

Slabs will then be heated to the required rolling temperature in the reheating furnace. Heated slabs will be extracted from the furnace by a slab discharging system to the discharge roller table. Discarded slabs will be transported through a reject roller table. Slabs will be transported by means of a roller table. The scale on the heated slabs will be removed in the primary descaler by spraying the jets of high-pressure water on both sides of the slab simultaneously.

After primary descaler, slabs will be rolled in a 4-Hi Reversing Roughing Mill to a transfer bar of required thickness at the entrance of finishing stands. There shall be provision of 2-Hi Reversing Roughing Mill with Edger before 4-Hi Reversing Roughing Mill. Roughing Mill will be equipped with a vertical edger to ensure the required width of the transfer bars. A delay table with heat holding cover and cobble pusher will be provided after the roughing stand to accommodate the transfer bar. Then, head and tail end of transfer bar will be cropped by a crop shear situated after the delay table. A secondary descaler will be provided before the entrance to the finishing stands to remove the scales of the transfer bar. Transfer bar will then be rolled to required final strip thickness in 4-Hi Finishing Mill train consisting of 7 stands. All roughing and finishing stands will be suitably equipped with the state-of-the-art technology including HAGC, loopers, work roll bending & shifting or pair cross rolling mechanism, fumes extraction system, automatic quick work roll changing etc. for production of smooth finished strip with required parameters. At the exit of finishing stands, suitable measuring devices shall be installed to measure the parameters of the rolled strip.

After the finishing stands, the rolled strip will pass through a run out table. A laminar cooling system with different cooling sections will be installed along the run-out table to cool the temperature of rolled strips to achieve the desired properties and coiling temperature of the strip. Thus, produced strip will then be coiled in the state-of-the-art Downcoilers. The rolled coils will then be transported to coil yard by means of a coil conveyor system installed in all the coil storage bays. Coil strapping, coil weighing, coil marking, off-line inspection will be provided for product packing and dispatch. Also, hot rolled coils will be fed to the cut-to-length line to produce hot rolled plates / sheets as required. Cut-to-length line will be located in one of the coil storages bays.

The erection and testing of 2 nos. walking beam type reheating furnaces shall be done in-line with the approved / RFI stamped drawings of Furnace and the installation / erection manual furnished by the Furnace supplier.
Various clauses of this Technical Specification as applicable will be adhered for erection of reheating furnace system components and auxiliaries.

There is a charging roller table in front of the furnace charging side, covering the entire width of the furnace. The slab’s weight, length and width are measured in front of the furnace. The slab is subsequently positioned by VVVF drive roller table. The available spaces inside the furnace is calculated by mathematical model. In case the space is available inside the furnace, the charging door opens and the slabs are charged into the reheating furnaces according to the mill rolling programme by overhead type charging machine. Then, the charging door is closed by hydraulic cylinders. The slab is transferred to fixed beams and thereafter conveyed towards the discharging side by walking beam mechanism. The speed of walking beam movement is controlled based on the charge history to ensure correct heating profile. The furnace temperature is controlled by fuel and air flow to achieve correct heating profile as per set points generated by mathematical model based on mill feedback, CV of gas, hot air temperature, oxygen content in flue gas etc. The walking beams and fixed beams are staggered at the discharging end to minimize skid marks. The walking beam movement is such that the end edge of slab at the discharge end shall be in the same position. After ensuring correct position as well as heating and soaking of the slab and readiness of the mill, the discharge doors opens and the overhead type extractors at the discharging end of furnaces picks up the slab from the furnaces and place it on the discharging roller table for onward movement to mill.

The waste gases leave the furnace through down take at the charging end and through flue tunnel finally escape in the atmosphere through self-supported chimney. Recuperator installed in the flue tunnel recovers waste heat and preheat combustion air before supplying the same to the burners.

The furnace shall be refractory lined to achieve the desired skin temperature. Charging machines, door drive & walking beam mechanism and discharging machine shall be hydraulically operated with auto positioning feedback system by means of LVDT/LDT. Adequate equipment / instrument shall be provided for checking pollutants in waste gas. Silencers shall be provided to limit noise level wherever necessary.

Complete operation of the furnace shall have Level 1 & 2 along with necessary interfaces with internal / external automation system. Provision shall be made to operate the furnace in manual / semi-automatic modes also. The furnace operation shall be completely integrated with Hot Strip Mill.

The Hot strip Mill under this package will consist of the Reheating Furnaces, Mill stand equipment, Cut to Length line and Roll Shop equipment. Following equipment have been considered in this package. Detailed description of the equipment is mentioned in the subsequent chapters.
List of EOT cranes for HSM complex shall be as per the layout.
This specification also covers erection, assisting in testing and commissioning of all shop EOT Cranes and their Hoists envisaged in Hot Strip Mill complex along with all associated electrics.
**End of Clauses**

records.text: 7.4.1 The shall align all the equipment to the accuracy as indicated in the drawings/erection manual/instruction of supplier’s site engineer. On completion of the final alignment prior to grouting of the equipment, a certificate indicating alignment/levels with reference to the drawing and actual dimensions achieved shall be prepared by the for supplier's/client certification.

7.4.2 The shall also establish permanent, mutually agreed center lines and benchmarks at convenient locations for future reference of the plant personnel. These shall be handed over to purchaser in document form.
7.4.3 The shall check rough concrete levels, foundation bolt locations and levels and pockets for all equipment foundation based on certified civil drawings before start of the erection.

7.4.4 If any major discrepancy is observed in the dimensions, centerlines, levels, locations, etc. of the foundation or anchor bolts or other embedment, the same shall be brought to the notice of the Purchaser in writing. The shall rectify the same within a reasonable time.

7.4.5 The levels and alignment of all installed equipment and technological structures shall be ensured to be within such tolerances as are recommended in the respective package suppliers’s instruction / drawings and/or as instructed by Purchaser/ equipment supplier. The axes, levels, alignment and expansion movements of all equipment shall be carefully checked jointly with Purchaser/equipment Supplier, adjustments shall be carried out as required and shall be recorded and duly signed. Corrective measures, if any, shall be carried out by the and offered for recheck by Purchaser/ equipment supplier. The motors shall be aligned and coupled with the machines properly. All mounted electrics and piping on equipment and the equipment proper shall be installed by the . In cases where equipment mounted pipes have arrived in straight lengths, same shall be bent and erected on the equipment by .

7.4.7 After the equipment has been installed, lined, leveled and aligned, the foundation bolts shall be tightened and bolts/base plates shall be grouted. When the grout has thoroughly cured, the alignment shall be rechecked and rectified, if required.
records.section: 7.4.6 All equipment shall be checked for free movement by hand rotation and correctly aligned and installed.

records.text: 7.4.9 The primary requisite for grouting of equipment is to solidly fill the gap between the rough concrete foundation and bottom of equipment, so that the equipment is firmly held in its aligned condition.
records.section: 7.4.8 The concrete surface shall be cleaned from all foreign particles before grouting of the equipment.

records.text: 7.4.11 Supply of grouting material and grouting of all plant and equipment and technological structures (including supply and fixing of chemical bolts with chemical bonding material/ adhesive etc.) to a thickness as per site requirement shall be part of equipment erection & shall not be considered as separate work.
records.section: 7.4.10 The type of grouting material shall be as specified by the equipment supplier.

records.text: 7.4.12 Generally for all technological steel structures and equipment non-shrink grouting material which is higher in grade then respective concrete foundation quality shall be considered. Wherever heat resistance concrete is used grout material also should have suitable heat resistance properties. The shall furnish manufacturer's test certificates for supplies of grouting materials. The type of grouting material shall be as specified by the equipment supplier.
records.section: 7.4.10 The type of grouting material shall be as specified by the equipment supplier.

records.text: Reference lines both longitudinal & transverse direction and elevation shall be developed and installed by the as per the requirement of suppliers / purchaser. Master reference point shall be provided by purchaser for transfer and development of the other reference lines.

Additional axes and elevation points that are required for the erection must be derived by the from the axes and elevation points fixed by the purchaser. However, the correctness of these axes and levels shall be ensured by the before commencing erection work.

The reference axes & levels shall be marked on steel bolts firmly anchored into concrete foundation, so that there is no displacement/ settlement etc. of the members during the tenure of erection. Bolts shall be supplied by the .

The spacers required for aligning the machines must be made available by the in smooth condition and without burr. It is advisable to insert one spacer plate each to the right and to the left of an anchor bolt before mounting the machine. Spacers shall be placed securely on sound foundations and will require pre-levelling.

It is preferable not to use more than three spacer plates if possible. If thicker machine spacer is used, they shall be measured and made to the corresponding thickness. Where several spacers are used, they shall be joined together by welding (spring effect).

After alignment and firm tightening of the machines on their base plates, a measurement is taken of the erected machines and the major dimensions with regard to axes and elevations are noted as nominal and actual sizes. The records are submitted to purchaser/ equipment supplier and signed by both parties. The recorded machines are then released for grouting. The clearance for final grouting will be given by purchaser/ equipment supplier in writing.

For office and other facilities, the shall undertake construction and maintenance of his site office as required for the work including all necessary facilities like water, power, telephone, fax, internet facilities, etc. and arrange for maintaining in a neat manner of the area placed at the 's disposal and providing sufficient fencing, notice boards and lights to protect and warn other as may be considered necessary by the Purchaser.
shall provide accommodation and transport facilities to his/ her staff and workmen. Arrangement of drinking water at office and execution site for staff and workmen shall also be arranged by .

The shall set up and maintain a small Workshop inside the premises on a continuous basis to facilitate day-to-day work as required for erection and commissioning work at his own cost. The shall arrange by his own all tools and tackles, consumables, spares, etc. required for maintenance of this workshop. The details of the machines proposed shall be furnished by the in his offer. The shall provide and be responsible for watch and ward of the goods in his custody on round the clock basis.

The shall be responsible for any loss of any of the items from the erection site and shall compensate Purchaser for such loss, theft, robbery, burglary, fire etc. The shall provide sufficient fencing, notice boards and lights to protect and warn others as may be considered necessary by Purchaser/ Supplier. shall provide necessary fire protection facilities at site and fabrication yard.

shall mobilize all labours (highly skilled, skilled, semi-skilled & un-skilled) under various categories like; Tested Welders, high skilled fitters, fitters, riggers, fabricators, mason, certified welders, pipe fitters, instrument tube fitters, licensed electricians, mechanics, foreman, surveyors, competent & certified NDT testing personnel etc. as required for all works under the contract.

Adequate number of experienced engineers and supervisors for supervision of the erection, testing and commissioning etc. as required shall be posted at site by the . If purchaser finds that the engineers and supervisors are not suitable for the assigned job, shall take action for replacement. A team of expert from the equipment suppliers will be available at site for providing necessary supervision and guidance to the supervisors. Engineers and Supervisors shall be required to communicate in English language with the foreign suppliers.

The shall engage skilled and competent manpower as per particular trade and as per requirement for all works under this contract.The supervisory staff employed by the shall ensure proper progress of work and discipline on the part of the labours engaged by the and ensure that all jobs are carried out in a safe and proper manner.

The shall maintain proper records of the materials under his custody. The procedure for maintenance of records will be mutually settled with the Purchaser. This may be reviewed periodically to incorporate improvements in the methods of record keeping.

The shall install suitable no. of PCs and printers in his office. These PCs shall be fed with various data related to the scope of work of the . The shall generate various reports by using PCs. Input data and output reports shall be mutually discussed and settled with the purchaser. The shall submit the format of all record keeping document along with the tender.

The shall deploy all labour - skilled, semi-skilled and unskilled, riggers, mill wright fitters, certified welders, pipe fitters, instrument fitters, mechanics, licensed electricians, supervisors, foremen, erection and testing engineers etc. needed for proper erection and commissioning of the plant and equipment.
The shall arrange management of erection work with proper and adequate supervision by qualified personnel for ensuring progress of erection work and quality of workmanship.

SCOPE OF WORK

The scope of work shall cover receipt of items from stores (inside the plant unit), loading, site transportation, unloading, unpacking, proper & safe storage, watch & ward, pre-erection testing at site, installation, erection including welding, alignment, painting, testing, lubrication/ greasing required if any, preliminary acceptance test, commissioning, performance guarantee test, liquidation of all defects observed during erection, testing & commissioning of all Cranes and Repair trolleys (as per list enclosed as Annexure-I) envisaged in Hot Strip Mill (HSM) of upcoming 4.08 MTPA integrated steel plant complete with their structural, mechanical, mounted electrics like motor, brake with its rectifier panel, limit switch, etc. including electrics & standard accessories & attachments as covered under this specification and approved GA drawings & other drawings, document, manuals handed over during pre-bid/ erection stage.

All such lifting tackles shall have valid test certificates. These certificates may be inspected by the employer or any statutory agency from time to time.
The shall arrange slings, wire ropes, pallet to support test load wherever necessary, stop watches and other necessary equipment/instrument to carry out the work.

GENERAL INSTRUCTIONS ON CONSTRUCTION & ERECTION PLAN
General
The shall submit a construction/ erection plan with the tender for the equipment under his scope of erection.
The plan will outline:
The construction / erection techniques to be employed,
Resource planning,
Deployment of construction / erection machineries,
Deployment of manpower of specific trade and requisite skill,
Construction / erection material planning,
Sequencing of construction and erection to avoid accumulation / underutilization of resource and to achieve better progress,
Action plan for completing critical work.
Deployment of Manpower

The shall submit a list showing deployment of manpower of the following categories indicating number of personnel schedule and duration of their posting at site, educational background, experience, etc.
Engineers to be engaged for co-ordination, follow-up and expediting.
Engineers and supervisory personnel directly attached to site work i.e. erection, testing, commissioning and demonstration of performance guarantee values.
Bio-data of the site-in-charge
Organization (proposed) for implementation of the package.
Erection of Equipment, levelling and Alignment
The shall ensure that the crane and repair trolley shall be erected as per the tolerances specified in design basis of Cranes and Hoists (Doc. No. ISPX2-DBR-CH-001, Rev.0)

The shall place the bed plates, sole or sub sole plates on the foundations and shim up with the machined steel plates/ wedges until; the equipment is in level as checked with precision level instruments. Required machined steel wedges and shim stock shall be provided by the successful and used in sufficient numbers to avoid unsupported spans without any perceptible deflection.

The levels and alignment of all installed equipment shall be ensured to be within tolerance limits as recommended. The axes, levels, alignment and expansion movements of all equipment shall be carefully rechecked jointly with employer representative before trial operation.

All motor driven equipment shall be accurately levelled and aligned to specified tolerances. Equipment available with mounted motors which are aligned and coupled shall be re-checked for alignment after opening the coupling.
The shall carry out necessary welding, gas cutting, grinding work as required for erection/ alignment / clearance.

Brakes, limit switches, strikers, other mounted electrics and instrumentation shall be mounted on the equipment by drilling and tapping holes or by welding as per drawings of the cranes/ hoist’s supplier.

Minor rectification work on equipment/components during erection/ assembly / trials shall be carried out by the at site by gas cutting, welding, grinding, drilling and tapping new holes, etc., as a part of erection work. Modification of base frames/supports frames of mounted electrics to the extent of enlarging/ plugging of holes, drilling and tapping new holes shall be carried out by the as part of erection work.
Flexible couplings, variable speed or variable stroke drives and guards shall be installed as supplied by the cranes/hoist’s supplier.
All equipment shall be checked for free movement by hand rotation when correctly aligned and installed.

Once the equipment has been levelled and aligned accurately with respect to reference bench mark and axis a final check is to be made for full tightness of anchor bolts without disturbing the level and alignment. If required, necessary corrections are to be made for level and alignment.
Suitable machine packings shall be supplied and used for obtaining accurate alignment of the mechanism. All loose packings shall be tack welded after alignment.

The shall install couplings of equipment to specified cranes/hoists supplier’s tolerances and approval shall be obtained from employer’s representatives before final coupling of the equipment is completed.
Couplings shall be disassembled and re-coupled as required during the trial runs of any equipment and its drives.
All gears should have an easy run. After boxing up of the drive, a check shall be done for freeness.

Installation of half coupling of shafts shall be checked by scribing block or dial indicator for eccentricity or misalignment. While rotating the shaft, radial division and facial misalignment of the shaft coupling should not be more than 0.1 mm. In case of gear couplings, extreme care shall be taken to fit all oil seals. Checking of tightness of fittings of half coupling on shaft should be as per the drawing. This shall be checked by feeler gauge.
Installation of bushing, pulleys, gears, etc. on shaft shall be checked for correctness.
Packing, Shims, etc.:
Packing plates, taper wedges in pair, shims, etc. used for levelling the equipment to maintain the required levels as per the drawings.

Packing plates to be used for levelling and adjustment shall be of suitable material, as recommended by employer’s representative, cut from GI /copper/brass plates or sheets into size varying from 200 to 300 mm in length, 50 to 100 mm in width and 20 to 100 mm in height, depending upon the weight of the equipment.

All packing plates above 10 mm thick shall be machined on both sides. Packing plates/sheets/shims 10 mm thick and below shall be deburred and hand ground before using. Taper wedges shall be machined with slope of 1:50.
Shims for final levelling shall be chosen from 0.5 to 5 mm strips. Shims shall also be kept ready from various thickness of galvanised cold rolled sheets/ copper/brass.

Packing plates/sheets/shims shall be placed close to the foundation bolts and at both sides of the bolts extending upto the diameter of the bolts. Distance between the two packing shall not be more than 600 mm.
Miscellaneous:

All motors shall be aligned and coupled with the equipment properly. Proper alignment between the motor and the equipment shall be determined by use of dial indicator. All mounted electrics shall be installed by the .

Rigging, site fittings, size assembling, riveting, bolting, welding, gas cutting, drilling, tapping and installation of all equipment and fabricated materials shall be solely as per drawings/ direction of employer’s representative.
All corrective measures, if required, shall be carried out by the and equipment shall be offered for re-check to employer’s representative.

The anchor bolts are tightened with proper tightening torque wrench and assuring that the packing plates and shims are solid. During final alignment of the equipment, the shim packs should be checked for tightness. Levelling screws shall be released before tightening of foundation bolts, if applicable.

After the equipment has been levelled and aligned and the alignment needs to be re-checked due to some discrepancy in alignment of connected electrical drive, then the same shall be carried out by the . In case of any discrepancy, all rectifications shall be carried out by the .
The shall clean thoroughly all the parts to be assembled, lubricate & reassemble
The tests / checks to be conducted during erection shall as per the instructions of employer. The shall attend to all rectifications of erection defects.
The shall couple the electrical motor to the mechanical drive and also align and level the electrical equipment mounted on mechanical equipment.
Certain electrical items shall be available at site duly assembled with mechanical equipment. The shall arrange for their erection, testing and commissioning.
Flushing, first filling and subsequent topping up of oils and lubricants, wherever necessary shall be carried out by .

The shall carry out any dismantling, re-assembly and re- erection of previously erected equipment, if so required, on account of reasons like defective erection work or due to overall requirements of subsequently erected equipment. The shall ensure that the levels, axes, etc., based on which he is aligning the equipment are authenticated and as such work will be minimum. The shall not claim extra for any such re- work.
Lubrication and greasing of equipment
Bearing for drives and driven equipment shall be checked and cleaned as follows:
Sleeve bearings

Remove bearing caps and drain plugs in bearing housing, Flush out housing with kerosene and drain completely. Replace drain plugs and fill lubrication system with recommended lubricant for the particular unit.
Grease lubricated ball bearings

Remove bearing housing cover to ensure supply and good clean condition thereof. If grease shows any sign of deterioration, dirt or contamination by any foreign material, remove grease completely, bathe bearing in kerosene, wipe dry and repack with appropriate grease for particular unit.
Oil Lubricated ball bearings
Flush out housing and bearings thoroughly with kerosene and drain completely, Fill reservoir to proper level with recommended lubricant for particular unit.
The above processes shall be repeated often as directed during the trial operation of the plant.

The erector shall carryout any dismantling and re- assembly of erected equipment that may be required due to unforeseen reasons. This activity may have to be carried out more than once due to requirement at site.

ERECTION, TESTING & COMMISSIOINING:
Erection work involves assembly & alignment of equipment parts including motors and brakes of electrical components and installation of the equipment parts as per approved drawings.

The is to transport all the components from stores / unloading site to the proper location of the erection with their own arrangement. Un-packing of packages and listing of damaged / short supply items to be recorded and immediately reported to Cranes/hoists supplier's site in charge.

To maintain the inventory of items at site, the shall maintain detailed records of all the equipment received and issued from the stores under their control. Unloading and loading of equipment at stores and at erection site shall be the total responsibility of the .
The erection to be done in proper sequence to ease subsequent mounting, alignment, assemblies & subassemblies and safety of man & equipment.
Proper safety measures to be taken as to avoid accidents that may cause damage to material and injury to persons working.

The shall supply all consumable and auxiliary materials like soldering flux, electrodes, insulation materials, PVC tapes and other sundry materials needed for the completion of erection as per specification given by Cranes/hoists supplier.

All Cranes & Hoist structures, mechanical and electrical equipment shall be stored and handled in such a manner that they are not subjected to damage or excessively stressed due to mishandling and / or environmental conditions.
Erection and alignment of electrical items including motors, brakes, electrics items bases.
Initial requirement of lubricant including replenishment during erection and commissioning work shall be informed by the .
To & fro transportation of load for load testing, arranging sling, cradle/platform etc. shall be arranged by the . However, required load for load testing of cranes shall be provided by employer.

The Positioning, leveling and plumbing of the crane girders end carriages, long travel drivers, etc. shall be carried out in the pre-assembly yard in accordance with the approved drawings. For any item of trolley not supplied in assembled condition, the same shall be positioned, leveled and plumbed in the pre -assembly yard in accordance with the approved drawings. The work shall be carried out to the satisfaction of the employer. For the purpose of assembly open space will be provided by the employer subject to availability at site.

The shall carry out the work in accordance with relevant IS, IPSS standards, Indian electricity rules and the drawing data information, instructions furnished by the cranes/hoists’s supplier. The testing and commissioning of the cranes/hoists shall be done in presence of the representatives of the cranes/hoist’s supplier, consultant and the end user.

While conducting tests on the crane/hoist, the shall not be held responsible for any manufacturing short comings of shops but shall be held responsible for defective workmanship carried out during the erection work. The shall arrange slings, wire ropes, stop watches and other necessary equipment/ instrument to carryout the work. The shall be responsible for transporting, loading/ unloading of test loads from other shops of ISP, SAIL to the test site and return them back to the respective shops after the load test is over. The shall also be responsible to stack the test loads suitably for load test.

Acceptance Test parameters to be achieved by the during PG and acceptance test. The erection, alignment & assembly should be such that all the technical parameters as given in the drawing are properly met. Cranes/hoists supplier shall actively help in achieving the test parameters.
PROPOSED ERECTION PROCEDURE:
Erection work involves assembly of equipment parts including electrical components and installation of the equipment parts as per approved drawings.

During erection, the steel work/equipment shall be securely bolted or properly fastened and when necessary shall be temporarily braced to take care of all loads transferred to the structure during erection including those due to erection equipment and its operation.
No permanent bolting or welding should be done until proper alignment has been made & the same is duly inspected & cleared by authorized inspecting agency.
Erection and dismantling of equipment parts shall be carried out in a manner so as to ensure stability of the crane structure at all stages of erection.

The shall be held responsible for the assembly of the equipment parts within the required accuracy. The dimension, of the crane/hoist after assembly shall be within the tolerances as specified. All materials shall be straight unless required to be of curvilinear form and shall be free from twists. All cold straightening shall be done by pressure only.
As far as possible sub-assembly shall carried out on the ground.

Instrumental checking for correctness of alignment of the equipment shall be carried out by the . The necessary instrument shall be arranged by the . Details of alignments done at site will have to be submitted by .

Bolting at site shall be carried out with the same accuracy and care as those adopted in the shop. When parts are to be joined by bolts, these shall be tightened to the maximum limit. The threaded portion of each bolt shall project through the nut by at least one thread. Bolts shall be fitted with spring washer. Where fitted bolts are required, new bolts shall be used with holes re-reamed to the required tolerances.

Welding- Only qualified welders shall be employed to carry out welding process. The welding shall be uniform and free from defects like slag inclusion, blow holes porosity, cracks, under cuts etc. There shall not be over lapping or undercutting of the parent metal.

Necessary adjustment to the structure, miscellaneous rectification of structure, wheels, equipment etc. for satisfactory operation and acceptance of the equipment shall be made by . Any modification, rectification or repairs to members or equipment which have been damaged during transit have to be made good by the to ensure proper assembly of the components. Any modification or repair to members or equipment which have been damaged during storage and handling or otherwise, shall be made good by the free of cost, to ensure proper assembly of the component.

Erection of the equipment and its electrical and mechanical components shall be carried out as per approved erection instructions/documents. Proper care shall be taken so that pipes, electrical wiring etc. are not damaged due to faulty or careless dismantling and erection procedure. The employer decision shall be final regarding any damage due to wrong dismantling or faulty erection procedure and the entire cost of replacement of the damaged part shall be borne by the .

The plan of erection of various motors shall be seen from the relevant diagrams. The equipment may be mounted on base frames. The base frames of all motors shall be laid at the appropriate place provided for the purpose and shall be carefully aligned with the help of leveling instruments and weld properly. Base frames shall also be coated with suitable paints as done for other equipment.
ERECTION SEQUENCE:

may visit the site to see the site condition & to have an idea of similar existing system for better understanding. Keeping in view the overall time schedule, the has to submit the sequence of erection along with realization schedule by the .
TESTS AT SITE BY ERECTION
The cranes/ hoists after erection shall be tested as specified in design basis of Cranes and Hoists (Doc. No. ISPX2-DBR-CH-001, Rev.0)
The crane/hoist shall be offered for inspection and testing during different stages of its erection.
When conducting tests, erector shall provide his own crane operator. However, the employer shall be entitled to employ his own crane operator, if he so desires.
TOUCH UP PAINTING

Before transportation of the equipment from employer’s store to erection site, necessary cleaning, brushing, Shop coats of rust inhibiting paints, lacquers etc. shall be applied to various parts, if necessary.
Damaged and defective shop coats shall be touched up with the same type of paint as used for shop coat.
Requisite quantity of the paints shall be supplied by the cranes/ hoist’s supplier of the equipment.
Before commencing painting of the steel which is delivered unpainted, all surfaces to be painted shall be dry and thoroughly cleaned from all loose mill scales, rust and foreign matters.
After erection the damaged portion occurred due to transport or erection or welding works shall be retouched and then one final coast shall be applied with primer.
Colour scheme shall be as per design basis for painting specification (Doc. No. ISPX2-DBR-PT-001).
SERVICES TO BE PROVIDED BY CRANES/HOISTS SUPPLIER/OEM (EXCLUDED FROM ’S SCOPE)
The following will be in the scope of cranes/hoists supplier/OEM apart from that specifically mentioned against each equipment:
Supply of complete equipment to be erected at Site
Supply of all drawings/documents required for erection, testing & commissioning of the equipment.
Supervision of erection, testing and commissioning and conducting various tests at site as required.
SAFETY PRECAUTIONS DURING ERECTION
General
It will be the principal function of the site in - charge of the to organise a group with qualified engineers primarily with a view to ensure the safety of all concerned people during the erection.
Broad functions of this group are enumerated below:
- Grading of work situations with regard to their propensity for safety.
- Identifying areas of work needing safety measures to be undertaken.
- Devise appropriate safety methodologies for follow - up by all concerned with regard to national codes/ statutory regulations.
- Work out provision of minimum quantum of safety appliances as applicable to a variety of work situations for follow - up.

- Periodically check the working conditions in various erection zones with regard to noise level, ventilation provisions, level of environmental contamination, fire hazards, concentration of heavy erection equipment in work areas, etc. and to recommend/enforce suitable safety measures.
Enforcement of safety regulations on every erection agency
- To ensure that the agencies are aware of the appropriate safety provisions to be made by them.
- To ensure that safety measures are strictly followed.
- To ensure that availability of first aid facilities, along with qualified and trained personnel to administer the same.
- Ensure compliance with national codes/ statutory regulations relating to safety as applicable by all agencies concerned during erection.
- Procurement of various safety appliance, proper knotting of chains, ropes, etc.,
- Periodical checking of all mechanisms and auxiliaries for performance, etc.
- Proper storage of gas cylinders from sources of heat, correct handling of cylinders both during and after use, use of goggles during welding, provision of incombustible screen, etc.
- Clearance of all personnel from work area during shifting of equipment before and after assembly as well as movement.
- Necessary barricading/guarding of areas where critical tests/ erection, etc. may be in progress.

records.text: During welding of pipes having carbon content less than 0.25 %, for pipes 20 mm and below, minimum temperature of the parent metal shall not be less than 5 degree C and for pipes above 20 mm, minimum temperature of parent metal shall not be less than 200 degree C. This minimum temperature shall be maintained through out the welding period on a particular welding joint. However there shall be no pipe with thickness of 20 mm and above.
records.section: 09. TECHNICAL SPECIFICATION FOR ERECTION OF HYDRAULIC, OIL LUBRICATION, GREASE LUBRICATION, DESCALER, AND PNEUMATIC SYSTEMS WITH INTERCONNECTING PIPING & OTHER FACILITIES

records.text: Welding joints shall be sound and free of any welding defects. Welding consumables for any fabrication work shall be of approved make and quality. Weld-edge preparation, type of welding, position of welding, cleaning of welding slag between passes, marking and weld profile etc shall be as per approved welding practice. Any welding joint, on visual inspection, if found not conforming to the requirement shall be liable for rejection. The contractor shall thereupon take necessary corrective measures to ensure proper quality. The contractor shall make necessary arrangement and provide facilities for carrying out tests for welding defects. Edge preparation for welding joints shall be done as per ANSI- B-16.9-1993. For Hydraulic, descaling and Circulating Oil Systems (All seamless pipes) butt welding joints root run shall be done by Tungsten Inert Gas (TIG) welding and filling shall be done by electric arc welding. Butt weld of all SS pipes shall also have root run by TIG welding and filling by electric arc welding. Pipes 50 NB and below shall be joined by socket weld fittings.
records.section: 09. TECHNICAL SPECIFICATION FOR ERECTION OF HYDRAULIC, OIL LUBRICATION, GREASE LUBRICATION, DESCALER, AND PNEUMATIC SYSTEMS WITH INTERCONNECTING PIPING & OTHER FACILITIES

records.text: After welding stress relieving shall be carried out as per ANSI B 31.1-1995.

records.text: Pipe bending machine shall be used. Pipe bends so made shall be true to angle and have a smooth surface free of flat corners and corrugations and shall have a radius of 3 times the nominal diameter unless otherwise specified. Actual inside dia at any portion of the bend shall not deviate by more than allowed. Hot bending shall not be allowed.

records.text: The contractor shall use welding electrodes of compatible material for welding of pipe and supports etc. The make of welding electrodes shall be L&T/GMM (Morgardshammer)/AdvaniOerlikon. For any specific application if the proper electrode is not available from above makes, the contractor may use electrodes of other makes only after getting prior approval from Client/consultant.

records.text: Hydrostatic test pressure of all the piping including lubrication, hydraulic, water, grease, air, gas lines shall be carried out at test pressure as mentioned in the drawing or else 1.5 times the system pressure if test pressure is not mentioned. The hydrostatic testing shall be carried out with water by hand /motorised pump of adequate capacity. Welding joints shall be subjected to hand hammer test while under test pressure. Duration of test shall be minimum 15 min.

records.text: The contractor shall deploy experienced and resourceful sub contractor exclusively for pickling and flushing of hydraulics and lubricating oil pipe. Before start of pickling and flushing, the contractor shall supply Pickling and flushing procedure to the purchaser for approval and shall undertake pickling and flushing as per the approved procedure. If there is any procedure provided by the PT, the same shall be implemented for pickling and flushing of pipelines. All the hydraulic, oil lubrication and in some cases grease lubrication piping shall be INSITU pickled and flushed. In addition to pickling and flushing procedure, for final INSITU cleaning / flushing for other pipes such as water and air pipes not requiring pickling and flushing shall be provided by the contractor for approval of purchaser. The approved procedure shall be followed by the contractor for cleaning and flushing of water and air pipelines.
records.section: 09. TECHNICAL SPECIFICATION FOR ERECTION OF HYDRAULIC, OIL LUBRICATION, GREASE LUBRICATION, DESCALER, AND PNEUMATIC SYSTEMS WITH INTERCONNECTING PIPING & OTHER FACILITIES

records.text: The piping contractor shall supply enough tanks, pumps for pickling, flushing skids consisting of pumps, hose connections, heating facilities, filters of different ratings, wire mesh sheets, valves and small hammers to permit processing of two or more systems simultaneously as required. The wire mesh sheets of adequate mesh size for pickling and flushing shall be supplied by the contractor. However, filter elements for final flushing through system filters shall be supplied by the PT as free issue items to the contractor. Tank / bath pickling of smaller lengths if any removed from the piping net work shall be done while the system is being pickled by circulation.
records.section: 09. TECHNICAL SPECIFICATION FOR ERECTION OF HYDRAULIC, OIL LUBRICATION, GREASE LUBRICATION, DESCALER, AND PNEUMATIC SYSTEMS WITH INTERCONNECTING PIPING & OTHER FACILITIES

records.text: A working time schedule shall be prepared by the contractor and submitted to PT/Consultant/Client for actual work plan. The date of start of erection shall be intimated to the contractor in advance. The contractor shall reschedule the work and work for extra hours at his own cost as may be necessary to meet the required erection schedule. For timely completion of all the activities, the contractor shall deploy additional man power as and when required. The Contractor shall deploy all labour - skilled, semi-skilled and unskilled, riggers, mill wrights, certified welders, pipe fitters, instrument fitters, mechanics, licensed electricians, supervisors, foremen, erection and testing engineers etc. needed for proper erection and commissioning of the plant and equipment. The Contractor shall arrange management of erection work with proper and adequate supervision by qualified personnel for ensuring progress of erection work and quality of workmanship.

records.section: 09. TECHNICAL SPECIFICATION FOR ERECTION OF HYDRAULIC, OIL LUBRICATION, GREASE LUBRICATION, DESCALER, AND PNEUMATIC SYSTEMS WITH INTERCONNECTING PIPING & OTHER FACILITIES

records.text: The trays shall be earthed and rendered electrically continuous by welding the trays to the grounding strip at not less than two places from both sides of the tray. The earthing strip shall be joined with the main earthing grid at least at two points. This shall be a part of cable tray erection.
records.section: 09. TECHNICAL SPECIFICATION FOR ERECTION OF HYDRAULIC, OIL LUBRICATION, GREASE LUBRICATION, DESCALER, AND PNEUMATIC SYSTEMS WITH INTERCONNECTING PIPING & OTHER FACILITIES

records.text: In some cases cables have to be laid through GI pipes. The has to lay the GI pipes and pull boxes. Conduits and Pull boxes must be connected to the earth grid. ’s scope includes laying of pipes/conduits, providing required bends as per site requirement. Sharp open end of the conduit has to be made smooth by filing or any other means to avoid damage of the cable sheath. End of conduits should be blocked / sealed. Conduits must be suitably clamped using saddle clamps at intervals of roughly 1 meter.
records.section: 09. TECHNICAL SPECIFICATION FOR ERECTION OF HYDRAULIC, OIL LUBRICATION, GREASE LUBRICATION, DESCALER, AND PNEUMATIC SYSTEMS WITH INTERCONNECTING PIPING & OTHER FACILITIES

records.text: Conduits/GI pipes shall be bent using conduit bending machines. to arrange for proper bending machines at site on 24 hour basis as well as dies for threading conduits of diameters and threads corresponding to the standards. Bends shall be free from cracks, crimps or other damage to the pipe or its coating. This shall be included in the scope of erection of GI pipes/conduits and no separate rate or additional charge shall be entertained for this.
records.section: 09. TECHNICAL SPECIFICATION FOR ERECTION OF HYDRAULIC, OIL LUBRICATION, GREASE LUBRICATION, DESCALER, AND PNEUMATIC SYSTEMS WITH INTERCONNECTING PIPING & OTHER FACILITIES

records.text: Ends of conduits shall be suitably grinded to remove rough edges. The threaded ends of conduits shall be painted with anticorrosive paint. The outer ends shall be smoothened free of burrs and sharp edges.
records.section: 09. TECHNICAL SPECIFICATION FOR ERECTION OF HYDRAULIC, OIL LUBRICATION, GREASE LUBRICATION, DESCALER, AND PNEUMATIC SYSTEMS WITH INTERCONNECTING PIPING & OTHER FACILITIES

records.text: Wire earthing shall be provided along the entire length of the conduits. Conduits shall be joined by threading and using joining sockets or conduit boxes or pull boxes. For this purpose, must have conduit bending machine and threading tools at site. Welding shall not be allowed for conduit jointing.
records.section: 09. TECHNICAL SPECIFICATION FOR ERECTION OF HYDRAULIC, OIL LUBRICATION, GREASE LUBRICATION, DESCALER, AND PNEUMATIC SYSTEMS WITH INTERCONNECTING PIPING & OTHER FACILITIES

records.text: Earthing terminals shall be provided at each conduit box.(except Fiber Optic & Bus Cables).
records.section: 09. TECHNICAL SPECIFICATION FOR ERECTION OF HYDRAULIC, OIL LUBRICATION, GREASE LUBRICATION, DESCALER, AND PNEUMATIC SYSTEMS WITH INTERCONNECTING PIPING & OTHER FACILITIES

records.text: All saddles, tubes and boxes shall be in perfect alignment to avoid any appearance of warping when the installation is complete. Saddles should not be so tight as to prevent expansion of the conduit. Steel conduits running on the surface shall be secured with galvanised spacer bar or distance type saddles. Saddles shall be spaced not more than 2m apart.
records.section: 09. TECHNICAL SPECIFICATION FOR ERECTION OF HYDRAULIC, OIL LUBRICATION, GREASE LUBRICATION, DESCALER, AND PNEUMATIC SYSTEMS WITH INTERCONNECTING PIPING & OTHER FACILITIES

records.text: Particular care shall be taken to ensure that no foreign material enters the conduit through joints or through surface openings. Jointing of conduits shall be done only in straight portion and not in bend portion. Screw holes in conduit boxes and the like must be kept entirely free and clean. Empty conduits left for future use shall be capped. At conduit terminations at plain holes in metal enclosures or in trunking, flanged couplers shall be used.
records.section: 09. TECHNICAL SPECIFICATION FOR ERECTION OF HYDRAULIC, OIL LUBRICATION, GREASE LUBRICATION, DESCALER, AND PNEUMATIC SYSTEMS WITH INTERCONNECTING PIPING & OTHER FACILITIES

records.text: Conduits buried in screed shall be installed without joints over the length of the conduit in the screed. Where this is not possible the shall ensure that the joints are adequately sealed without the conductivity of the conduit being impaired. Joints shall be painted with bituminous based paint.
records.section: 09. TECHNICAL SPECIFICATION FOR ERECTION OF HYDRAULIC, OIL LUBRICATION, GREASE LUBRICATION, DESCALER, AND PNEUMATIC SYSTEMS WITH INTERCONNECTING PIPING & OTHER FACILITIES

records.text: Flexible conduits shall be used during the final run of the cables being connected to the motors/ field devices etc. Flexible conduits must be fixed using end sockets and clamps. Flexible conduits shall be of heavy duty PVC coated type.
records.section: 09. TECHNICAL SPECIFICATION FOR ERECTION OF HYDRAULIC, OIL LUBRICATION, GREASE LUBRICATION, DESCALER, AND PNEUMATIC SYSTEMS WITH INTERCONNECTING PIPING & OTHER FACILITIES

records.text: ’s scope of work shall include making mounting provision and installation of instrumentation & control equipment; construction of all civil/ structural rooms along with necessary facilities for instrumentation & controls; fabrication & erection of maintenance platform & approach ladder and fabrication & erection of structures required for cable laying for instrumentation & control system. Detailed requirement of these rooms is indicated in clause no. 10.09.4.
records.section: 11 TECHNICAL SPECIFICATION FOR ERECTION WORKS OF INSTRUMENTATION & CONTROL (For Re-Heating Furnace)

records.text: ’s scope of work shall also include receipt of free issue items (instruments) and erection hardware (impulse pipes & tubes, impulse pipe fittings required for installation of instruments, electrical accessories, panels / cabinets and all associated hardware), transportation to site including loading, unloading, temporary storage of all free issue items; erection, testing (including Hydro testing of pressure tapping point) and commissioning of the instruments and control items. ’s scope shall include temporary storage of free issue items with watch and ward, distribution of construction power, temporary lighting required for erection work. During erection, instrumentation equipment will be handed over to the , time to time, as per the progress of work. The scope of work shall also include construction of site laboratory for testing & calibration of instruments before installation as well as during testing & commissioning of the plant, if required due to complain from client/ consultant/ technological pkg. contractor for any malfunctioning. ’s scope shall also include liquidation of defect points, participation in tests for establishment of plant performance guarantee (PG) and post commissioning activities till issue of final acceptance certificate (FAC) by Purchaser.

CIVIL JOBS FOR INSTRUMENTATION & CONTROL SYSTEM.
For Instrumentation & control system, two types of room shall be constructed, viz. Analyser rooms and Instrument laboratory cum area repair shop.
Analyzer rooms
The Analyser Room may please be constructed with facilities as indicated below:
Room shall be provided with metallic entry door.
The Analyser Room shall have standard double-walled sliding glass windows (2 nos. of size 2000(W)x 1300(H), one at front side & other at back side of room), open-able & lockable type.
The analyser room will have industrial grade package type air-conditioning system. The ACVS is under BOP package vendor’s scope. Hence, AHU room shall be provided at the side of each analyser room.

The gas cylinders shall be planned to kept outside the room. The Cylinder shall be open to atmosphere and under shed. The size of the shed shall be considered as 2500(L)x750(W). Also, grill shall be provided with lock & key arrangement, to place the gas cylinder in a secure place.

Depth & width of the trench shall be considered as 500mm. The trench shall be provided in the room from one wall to another wall. Cables shall be laid through trench. MS insert plate of size 100x100x8 mm @500mm shall be provided (100 mm above bottom of trench) at both sides of trench for laying of cable trays. For fixing of cable trays, angles (50x50x6 mm) shall be fixed with the above referred insert plates. Around 50% of the trench shall be covered with removable type MS chequered plates.

Necessary opening, plate inserts, pipes etc. shall be provided for entry of sample gas lines, purging air/ nitrogen line, test gas line, venting gas line and for cable entry. Following pipe inserts shall be considered as minimum:
The analyser panel room will be provided with exhaust fan with louver. Necessary opening (12”) shall be kept for installation of the same.

records.text: For steam or vapour at temperature greater than 120C, condensation chamber shall be used. Condensation chambers shall be installed as near to tapping point as possible. Condensation chambers shall be IBR certified for steam application, which comes under IBR guideline.

records.text: For installation of impulse tubes with compression fittings, proper care shall be taken for handling, bending of the tubes and tightening of the fittings. Standard instruction of the manufacturer shall be followed strictly for the above cases.

records.text: Pipes/tubes shall be bent using pipe/tube benders only. Hot bending of impulse pipes/tubes is prohibited. Impulse line bending shall be circular and smooth. Flat bending shall be rejected. Pipes/ tubes shall be cut using pipe cutting device. Hot cutting will not be allowed.
records.section: 2 nos., 50 mm dia. Medium grade GI pipe, at elevation +1300mm from FFL (above the gas cylinder mounting location), for entering calibration gas line.

records.text: All threaded joints shall be joined with Teflon tape and no other joining component shall be used except on high temperature service, where graphite sealing components shall be used. Pipe threading shall be made using suitable threading machine.
records.section: 2 nos., 50 mm dia. Medium grade GI pipe, at elevation +1300mm from FFL (above the gas cylinder mounting location), for entering calibration gas line.

records.text: Impulse lines shall be properly supported and shall be clamped with the support. Impulse lines shall never be welded with the support, metal constructions, equipment or structure of building. Supports shall not generally be taken from process pipes, equipment, handrails etc.

records.text: Specification for welding of piping
records.section: 2 nos., 50 mm dia. Medium grade GI pipe, at elevation +1300mm from FFL (above the gas cylinder mounting location), for entering calibration gas line.

records.text: Components to be welded shall be properly aligned, spaced and fitted up so as to hold the edges during welding at proper distance to obtain full root penetration. Any misalignment between the edges to be welded shall not exceed 1.5 mm, and care shall be taken to distribute it along the circumference. Should the misalignment be over 1.5 mm, the end of the pipe with the smaller ID shall be internally machines or ground, so as to reduce the misalignment to the prescribed value. The machined area shall be tapered with a slope not greater than1:3.
records.section: 2 nos., 50 mm dia. Medium grade GI pipe, at elevation +1300mm from FFL (above the gas cylinder mounting location), for entering calibration gas line.

records.text: Any surface irregularities shall be grounded off and filed.
records.section: 2 nos., 50 mm dia. Medium grade GI pipe, at elevation +1300mm from FFL (above the gas cylinder mounting location), for entering calibration gas line.

records.text: For fillet welds (slip-on flanges, socket welds, etc) tack welding shall be permitted, using the electrodes allowed for the weld execution, with preheating when required for the pipe welds. The tacks shall be removed or molten during welding. In the latter case, the tacks shall be checked to ensure that they are free from defects. In socket welds, a gap of 2 mm between the end of the pipe and the socket bottom shall be provided.
records.section: 2 nos., 50 mm dia. Medium grade GI pipe, at elevation +1300mm from FFL (above the gas cylinder mounting location), for entering calibration gas line.

records.text: In general, process pipes are of carbon steel or mild-steel. For details, refer piping details included elsewhere in the TS. However, in some special application, stainless steel pipes will be used (eg. Oxygen service). Proper welding rod shall be used for welding on SS pipe.
records.section: 2 nos., 50 mm dia. Medium grade GI pipe, at elevation +1300mm from FFL (above the gas cylinder mounting location), for entering calibration gas line.

records.text: Arc welding shall be done for all carbon steel pipes, trays, supports, stanchions, supports, CS pipes, etc.

records.text: All finished welds shall be visually inspected for parallel and axial misalignment of the work, excessive reinforcement, concavity of welds, shrinkage, cracks, inadequate penetration, unprepared burn through under cuts, dimensions of the weld, surface porosity and other surface defects.

records.text: CALIBRATION
records.section: 2 nos., 50 mm dia. Medium grade GI pipe, at elevation +1300mm from FFL (above the gas cylinder mounting location), for entering calibration gas line.

records.text: shall check the calibration, functions, set points etc. of all the required instruments strictly as per manufacturer’s instructions in his site laboratory, prior to installation and also subsequently after installation, as & when required. shall make necessary arrangements for to & fro movements of such instruments (and also others, if so required by Client) between his office-cum-lab, stores and installation site, provide his manpower, prepare & submit calibration & test results. Details of instrumentation equipment are indicated in “Schedule of activities”.
records.section: 2 nos., 50 mm dia. Medium grade GI pipe, at elevation +1300mm from FFL (above the gas cylinder mounting location), for entering calibration gas line.

records.text: The instruments envisaged to be tested and calibrated in ’s site lab. are: Pressure & Differential Pressure Transmitters; Flow Transmitters (DPT); Level Transmitters (DPT), Temperature Elements (RTDs & Thermocouples); Pressure/ differential pressure switches (set point checking); Pressure gauges, Temperature Gauges, Temperature Transmitters; Control valves with actuators, E/P positioners & other accessories (100 % for all).
records.section: 2 nos., 50 mm dia. Medium grade GI pipe, at elevation +1300mm from FFL (above the gas cylinder mounting location), for entering calibration gas line.

records.text: Level transmitters (radar, ultrasonic, guided wave radar), flow meters (magnetic flow meters, vortex, coriolis) and anaylsers shall not be calibrated at site. It shall be checked with respect to its range using HART configurator.
records.section: 2 nos., 50 mm dia. Medium grade GI pipe, at elevation +1300mm from FFL (above the gas cylinder mounting location), for entering calibration gas line.

records.text: Calibration checks/ re-calibration of any instrument shall be done at any stage during the loop checking & commissioning, PAC & FAC if required to establish/confirm its satisfactory performance.
records.section: 2 nos., 50 mm dia. Medium grade GI pipe, at elevation +1300mm from FFL (above the gas cylinder mounting location), for entering calibration gas line.

records.text: All analog instruments shall be calibrated at 0%, 25%, 50%, 75% & 100% of the measured ranges in both ascending and descending orders. Discrete instruments, switching devices shall be functionally checked for general performance and specifically, for assigned set points.
records.section: 2 nos., 50 mm dia. Medium grade GI pipe, at elevation +1300mm from FFL (above the gas cylinder mounting location), for entering calibration gas line.

records.text: All calibration data and results shall be recorded in proper formats and submitted to Client/ consultant for record/review. It shall be signed by person calibrating the instruments, supervisor/ engineer of , consultant & client’s engineer, if required as per protocol followed by client. In case, deviations from specifications are observed, the matter shall be brought to the immediate notice of for necessary action.

records.text: The erection work shall be carried out according to the sound established engineering practice and as per the drawings and specifications supplied by the Purchaser. The Successful shall carry out the work in the presence and / or as per the instructions of the site engineer/ supervisory personnel deputed by the Purchaser. The erection work shall be carried out by highly skilled workforce. The Successful shall not remove any engineer, erector or skilled labour from the site without prior written approval of the Purchaser.
