The shall be responsible for any loss of any of the items from the erection site and shall compensate Purchaser for such loss, theft, robbery, burglary, fire etc. The shall provide sufficient fencing, notice boards and lights to protect and warn others as may be considered necessary by Purchaser/ Supplier. shall provide necessary fire protection facilities at site and fabrication yard.

shall mobilize all labours (highly skilled, skilled, semi-skilled & un-skilled) under various categories like; Tested Welders, high skilled fitters, fitters, riggers, fabricators, mason, certified welders, pipe fitters, instrument tube fitters, licensed electricians, mechanics, foreman, surveyors, competent & certified NDT testing personnel etc. as required for all works under the contract.

Adequate number of experienced engineers and supervisors for supervision of the erection, testing and commissioning etc. as required shall be posted at site by the . If purchaser finds that the engineers and supervisors are not suitable for the assigned job, shall take action for replacement. A team of expert from the equipment suppliers will be available at site for providing necessary supervision and guidance to the supervisors. Engineers and Supervisors shall be required to communicate in English language with the foreign suppliers.

The shall engage skilled and competent manpower as per particular trade and as per requirement for all works under this contract.The supervisory staff employed by the shall ensure proper progress of work and discipline on the part of the labours engaged by the and ensure that all jobs are carried out in a safe and proper manner.

The shall maintain proper records of the materials under his custody. The procedure for maintenance of records will be mutually settled with the Purchaser. This may be reviewed periodically to incorporate improvements in the methods of record keeping.

The shall install suitable no. of PCs and printers in his office. These PCs shall be fed with various data related to the scope of work of the . The shall generate various reports by using PCs. Input data and output reports shall be mutually discussed and settled with the purchaser. The shall submit the format of all record keeping document along with the tender.

The shall deploy all labour - skilled, semi-skilled and unskilled, riggers, mill wright fitters, certified welders, pipe fitters, instrument fitters, mechanics, licensed electricians, supervisors, foremen, erection and testing engineers etc. needed for proper erection and commissioning of the plant and equipment.
The shall arrange management of erection work with proper and adequate supervision by qualified personnel for ensuring progress of erection work and quality of workmanship.
**End of Clauses**
General:
Handling and hoisting equipment will include various capacities of cranes, and repair trolleys for process & service requirement pertaining to Hot Strip Mill (HSM) as per list enclosed (Annexure-I).

shall be accompanied with general arrangement drawing for each crane, repair trolley, handling attachment, if any, showing the elevation, side view and plan of the crane along with information such as clearances, construction of bridge structure, hook approaches, height of lift, location of operator’s cabin with direction of view of operator, wheel base, over buffer dimension, buffer height, arrangement and disposition of all the drives and other equipment installed on the crane.
The handling and hoisting system shall be erected, tested & commissioned to meet the technical requirements covered under relevant specifications & drawings.
Erection & testing shall be carried out in presence of OEM of cranes /Third Party Inspection agency on the basis of drawings, specification & latest Indian standards & Quality assurance Plan.
Approval of various procedures submitted by the shall not relieve him of his responsibilities towards completion of the work as defined in the contract.
furnish the following to Employer before starting of erection:
Erection plan schedule including list of personnel and erection equipment to be deployed.
A time bar chart showing starting and completion of various activities of erection of the Cranes & Hoists.

All the equipment structures, mechanical and electrical equipment shall be stored and handled in such a manner that they are not subjected to damage or excessive stress due to mishandling and environmental condition.

The positioning, leveling and plumbing of the equipment shall be carried out in accordance with the approved drawings. If the units are not supplied in assembled condition due to transportation reasons etc., all the items/parts shall be positioned, leveled and plumbed at ground in accordance with the approved drawings. The work shall be carried out to the satisfaction of the employer.
The shall be solely responsible for obtaining necessary clearance from various statutory bodies during execution of the work.

The shall carry out the work in accordance with relevant IS standards, and drawings, data, information, instructions furnished by the crane/hoist ‘s supplier. The testing and commissioning of the equipment shall be done in presence of the representative cranes/hoists supplier and employer/ consultants.
The suitability and capacity of all plant and equipment used for erection, testing and commissioning shall be to the satisfaction of the employer.
While conducting test of the equipment, the shall not be held responsible for any manufacturing defect but shall be held responsible for defective workmanship carried out during the erection work.
After completion of the work, the shall promptly remove all materials, equipment, and other items brought to the site and shall leave the premises in a clean and acceptable condition.
During course of erection work, the shall strictly follow all the safety norms/rules and regulations and shall abide by all the statutory rules and regulations.

Necessary drawings and documents required for erection made available to the employer by the cranes/ hoists supplier shall be provided. shall return the drawings and documents after completion of the scope of work.
should follow the safety rules and regulation as per ISP, SAIL requirements.
SCOPE OF WORK

The scope of work shall cover receipt of items from stores (inside the plant unit), loading, site transportation, unloading, unpacking, proper & safe storage, watch & ward, pre-erection testing at site, installation, erection including welding, alignment, painting, testing, lubrication/ greasing required if any, preliminary acceptance test, commissioning, performance guarantee test, liquidation of all defects observed during erection, testing & commissioning of all Cranes and Repair trolleys (as per list enclosed as Annexure-I) envisaged in Hot Strip Mill (HSM) of upcoming 4.08 MTPA integrated steel plant complete with their structural, mechanical, mounted electrics like motor, brake with its rectifier panel, limit switch, etc. including electrics & standard accessories & attachments as covered under this specification and approved GA drawings & other drawings, document, manuals handed over during pre-bid/ erection stage.
The scope of work of the shall also include the following for all Cranes and Hoists: -
The successful shall deploy adequate skilled and unskilled manpower and erection equipment for carrying out round the clock work at all the fronts to complete the project within specified schedule.

The necessary lifting tackles, tools, wire rope, slings of suitable capacities, testing equipment and other equipment to carry out work shall be arranged by the . All such lifting tackles shall have valid test certificates. These certificates may be inspected by the employer or any statutory agency from time to time.
The shall arrange slings, wire ropes, pallet to support test load wherever necessary, stop watches and other necessary equipment/instrument to carry out the work.

The shall arrange necessary equipment and light fittings with lamps and other accessories to adequately illuminate his work site. Maintenance of light fittings and replacement of fused lamps shall be 's responsibility.

Mobilization of required unskilled manpower and hydra/machinery for load handling required for crane/ repair trolley load testing at site. However, skilled manpower, technician and expert as per requirement, cradles/ cages and structural load for testing for cranes and repair trolleys are excluded from the scope of .

Making suitable level ground at erection site for receipt and unloading of material/equipment at site with their own men, crane, tools and tackles and making store for storing all the supply items properly at site to be allocated by ISP, SAIL. s are advised to visit site before quoting so that they are acquainted with site conditions and requirements.

The heavy structural components like Crane girders to be unloaded at erection site at respective bays where the item is to be erected, whereas the packed boxes, electrical components and other smaller components to be unloaded and stored in ’s store.

The shall store all the items properly and protect them from damage by weather, any other natural causes or stealing/theft, misuse till the equipment’s are handed over to the end user after satisfactory performance & acceptance test. For this purpose, shall construct a suitable store at erection site to protect valuable equipment’s from rain. The shall be responsible for any loss, fire, theft or burglary of any of the items from the stores and at erection site. The storage responsibility of the shall be over only when the plant and equipment are formally taken over by ISP, SAIL. Place shall be provided by employer within the Plant premises.

In case apparent damages/ shortages in consignments, packing are noticed by the , such cases shall be brought to the notice of Cranes/hoists supplier's site representative immediately and cleared only with their knowledge/ approval.

The shall arrange for handling and transportation of items / components from own store to the erection site including multiple handling, if required, as per the requirement of erection & commissioning.

All handling and transportation of erection and testing of equipment, fabricated structures at site, disposal of debris etc. including double handling as required for completion of the work in accordance with time schedule are deemed to be included in the scope of work of the .
Transportation of components of cranes and repair trolleys from storage premises/ shed at site to erection site.
The shall be responsible for transporting, loading/unloading of test loads to test site from other places within plant boundary.
The shall be responsible for arrangement of adequate material handling equipment like mobile cranes, trucks, trailers, fork lifters, tools and tackles as may be required for his work.
The shall be responsible for all damages to his trucks/ trailers/ cranes which may take place during the course of execution of work.

The shall make provision for adequate storage sheds which are to be wind and water-tight for the storage of necessary equipment, such as motors, brakes, cables, etc. to protect them from damp and moisture. All auxiliary and loose parts shall be systematically classified for ready identification and location whenever needed. As far as possible, materials shall be stored in the order in which they will be erected.
The shall provide and be responsible for watch and ward of the goods in his custody on round the clock basis. The shall be solely responsible for the safety and security of the stores.

The shall supply all consumable and auxiliary items like scaffolding materials, soldering flux, electrodes, insulation material, PVC tapes, grease and lubricants, cotton waste and other sundry materials etc. needed for the completion of work as per scope of work in Technical Specification.
Supply and provision of packing plates shims up to thickness 0.5-5 mm etc. required during erection and alignment of motors, brakes etc.
Conduct all tests under the supervision of equipment Cranes/hoists supplier/OEM as required by project / employer to obtain final acceptance certificate (FAC).

The scope of the shall include erection of all buffer stoppers and end stoppers of each cranes and repair trolleys. However, the supply of all buffer stoppers and end stoppers of each cranes and repair trolleys is covered under scope of Pkg. No. HSM-03.

The supply and installation of complete rail tracks for long travel (LT) motion of all cranes. The supply of clamps, insert plates etc. as required for rail fixing for long travel (LT) motion of all cranes is also covered in scope of .
The supply and installation of rail tracks for cross travel (CT) motion of all repair trolleys.

A tool box containing all necessary tools like torque wrench, portable hydraulic jack, hand grease gun, set off spanners, set of screw drivers, calipers, set off hammers,steelscale etc. Required for the erection and testingcrane and repair trolley.

Maintenance platform along with approaching stairs for all cranes and repair trolleys. However, Crane/ Repair trolleys mounted platform and approaching stairs/ ladders are excluded from the scope of .
shall note that during execution of the project if any existing facility/ equipment/ component are damaged by the , the same shall be made good by the free of cost by his own men and material.

The successful will be totally responsible for completing the total scope of work & its integration, and demonstrations of performance in close co-ordination and under the supervision of Cranes/hoists supplier/OEM.

Deputation of Safety Officer, use of safety net during erection of Cranes & Hoists, training of workers in safety etc. are in the scope of as it is basic requirement for execution of the erection & commissioning work.
For the scope of work related to Electrical system and auxiliaries, please refer relevant chapter of this technical specification.
BATTERY LIMIT FOR MECHANICAL SYSTEM OF CRANES AND HOISTS

The erection, testing and commissioning of all Cranes and Repair trolleys under battery limit of Hot Strip Mill (as per list enclosed as Annexure-I) with its structural, mechanical and electrics, controls and standard accessories mentioned in this technical specification are in scope of . However, the following are excluded from the ’s scope w.r.t. cranes and hoists envisaged in Hot Strip Mill:
The supply of all cranes and repair trolleys covered under this Pkg. (HSM-03).
The supply and erection of cranes and hoists covered under balance of plant package (HSM-02) of Hot Strip Mill.
The design of rail tracks for long travel (LT) motion of all cranes.
The design of rail tracks for cross travel (CT) motion of all repair trolleys.
Skilled manpower, technician and expert as per requirement for testing of cranes and repair trolleys.
Crane/ Repair trolleys mounted platform.
Crane/ Repair trolleys mounted approaching stairs/ ladders
Supply of all the commissioning spares required for successful commissioning for all cranes and hoists.
Supply of required quantity of paints for intermediate, final coat and retouching.
Training for Electrical and Mechanical personnel of Employer.

The first third party load test, inspection after erection of each cranes and their maintenance repair trolleys. However, mobilization of unskilled manpower and hydra/machinery required for crane/ repair trolley first third-party load test, inspection is in scope of .
The supply of two (02) years normal operation and maintenance (O&M) Spares is not included in scope of .
GENERAL INSTRUCTIONS ON CONSTRUCTION & ERECTION PLAN
General
The shall submit a construction/ erection plan with the tender for the equipment under his scope of erection.
The plan will outline:
The construction / erection techniques to be employed,
Resource planning,
Deployment of construction / erection machineries,
Deployment of manpower of specific trade and requisite skill,
Construction / erection material planning,
Sequencing of construction and erection to avoid accumulation / underutilization of resource and to achieve better progress,
Action plan for completing critical work.
Deployment of Manpower

The shall submit a list showing deployment of manpower of the following categories indicating number of personnel schedule and duration of their posting at site, educational background, experience, etc.
Engineers to be engaged for co-ordination, follow-up and expediting.
Engineers and supervisory personnel directly attached to site work i.e. erection, testing, commissioning and demonstration of performance guarantee values.
Bio-data of the site-in-charge
Organization (proposed) for implementation of the package.
Erection of Equipment, levelling and Alignment
The shall ensure that the crane and repair trolley shall be erected as per the tolerances specified in design basis of Cranes and Hoists (Doc. No. ISPX2-DBR-CH-001, Rev.0)

The shall place the bed plates, sole or sub sole plates on the foundations and shim up with the machined steel plates/ wedges until; the equipment is in level as checked with precision level instruments. Required machined steel wedges and shim stock shall be provided by the successful and used in sufficient numbers to avoid unsupported spans without any perceptible deflection.

The levels and alignment of all installed equipment shall be ensured to be within tolerance limits as recommended. The axes, levels, alignment and expansion movements of all equipment shall be carefully rechecked jointly with employer representative before trial operation.

All motor driven equipment shall be accurately levelled and aligned to specified tolerances. Equipment available with mounted motors which are aligned and coupled shall be re-checked for alignment after opening the coupling.
The shall carry out necessary welding, gas cutting, grinding work as required for erection/ alignment / clearance.

Brakes, limit switches, strikers, other mounted electrics and instrumentation shall be mounted on the equipment by drilling and tapping holes or by welding as per drawings of the cranes/ hoist’s supplier.

Minor rectification work on equipment/components during erection/ assembly / trials shall be carried out by the at site by gas cutting, welding, grinding, drilling and tapping new holes, etc., as a part of erection work. Modification of base frames/supports frames of mounted electrics to the extent of enlarging/ plugging of holes, drilling and tapping new holes shall be carried out by the as part of erection work.
Flexible couplings, variable speed or variable stroke drives and guards shall be installed as supplied by the cranes/hoist’s supplier.
All equipment shall be checked for free movement by hand rotation when correctly aligned and installed.

Once the equipment has been levelled and aligned accurately with respect to reference bench mark and axis a final check is to be made for full tightness of anchor bolts without disturbing the level and alignment. If required, necessary corrections are to be made for level and alignment.
Suitable machine packings shall be supplied and used for obtaining accurate alignment of the mechanism. All loose packings shall be tack welded after alignment.

The shall install couplings of equipment to specified cranes/hoists supplier’s tolerances and approval shall be obtained from employer’s representatives before final coupling of the equipment is completed.
Couplings shall be disassembled and re-coupled as required during the trial runs of any equipment and its drives.
All gears should have an easy run. After boxing up of the drive, a check shall be done for freeness.

Installation of half coupling of shafts shall be checked by scribing block or dial indicator for eccentricity or misalignment. While rotating the shaft, radial division and facial misalignment of the shaft coupling should not be more than 0.1 mm. In case of gear couplings, extreme care shall be taken to fit all oil seals. Checking of tightness of fittings of half coupling on shaft should be as per the drawing. This shall be checked by feeler gauge.
Installation of bushing, pulleys, gears, etc. on shaft shall be checked for correctness.
Packing, Shims, etc.:
Packing plates, taper wedges in pair, shims, etc. used for levelling the equipment to maintain the required levels as per the drawings.

Packing plates to be used for levelling and adjustment shall be of suitable material, as recommended by employer’s representative, cut from GI /copper/brass plates or sheets into size varying from 200 to 300 mm in length, 50 to 100 mm in width and 20 to 100 mm in height, depending upon the weight of the equipment.

All packing plates above 10 mm thick shall be machined on both sides. Packing plates/sheets/shims 10 mm thick and below shall be deburred and hand ground before using. Taper wedges shall be machined with slope of 1:50.
Shims for final levelling shall be chosen from 0.5 to 5 mm strips. Shims shall also be kept ready from various thickness of galvanised cold rolled sheets/ copper/brass.

Packing plates/sheets/shims shall be placed close to the foundation bolts and at both sides of the bolts extending upto the diameter of the bolts. Distance between the two packing shall not be more than 600 mm.
Miscellaneous:

All motors shall be aligned and coupled with the equipment properly. Proper alignment between the motor and the equipment shall be determined by use of dial indicator. All mounted electrics shall be installed by the .

Rigging, site fittings, size assembling, riveting, bolting, welding, gas cutting, drilling, tapping and installation of all equipment and fabricated materials shall be solely as per drawings/ direction of employer’s representative.
All corrective measures, if required, shall be carried out by the and equipment shall be offered for re-check to employer’s representative.

The anchor bolts are tightened with proper tightening torque wrench and assuring that the packing plates and shims are solid. During final alignment of the equipment, the shim packs should be checked for tightness. Levelling screws shall be released before tightening of foundation bolts, if applicable.

After the equipment has been levelled and aligned and the alignment needs to be re-checked due to some discrepancy in alignment of connected electrical drive, then the same shall be carried out by the . In case of any discrepancy, all rectifications shall be carried out by the .
The shall clean thoroughly all the parts to be assembled, lubricate & reassemble
The tests / checks to be conducted during erection shall as per the instructions of employer. The shall attend to all rectifications of erection defects.
The shall couple the electrical motor to the mechanical drive and also align and level the electrical equipment mounted on mechanical equipment.
Certain electrical items shall be available at site duly assembled with mechanical equipment. The shall arrange for their erection, testing and commissioning.
Flushing, first filling and subsequent topping up of oils and lubricants, wherever necessary shall be carried out by .

The shall carry out any dismantling, re-assembly and re- erection of previously erected equipment, if so required, on account of reasons like defective erection work or due to overall requirements of subsequently erected equipment. The shall ensure that the levels, axes, etc., based on which he is aligning the equipment are authenticated and as such work will be minimum. The shall not claim extra for any such re- work.
Lubrication and greasing of equipment
Bearing for drives and driven equipment shall be checked and cleaned as follows:
Sleeve bearings

Remove bearing caps and drain plugs in bearing housing, Flush out housing with kerosene and drain completely. Replace drain plugs and fill lubrication system with recommended lubricant for the particular unit.
Grease lubricated ball bearings

Remove bearing housing cover to ensure supply and good clean condition thereof. If grease shows any sign of deterioration, dirt or contamination by any foreign material, remove grease completely, bathe bearing in kerosene, wipe dry and repack with appropriate grease for particular unit.
Oil Lubricated ball bearings
Flush out housing and bearings thoroughly with kerosene and drain completely, Fill reservoir to proper level with recommended lubricant for particular unit.
The above processes shall be repeated often as directed during the trial operation of the plant.

The erector shall carryout any dismantling and re- assembly of erected equipment that may be required due to unforeseen reasons. This activity may have to be carried out more than once due to requirement at site.
DRAWINGS AND INSTRUCTIONS
The shall be furnished with soft copy of drawings for study, guidance and compliance in completing the installation of all equipment to the satisfaction of employer’s representative.

The shall be responsible for the preparation of detailed erection marking plans, drawings for pipe supports, if any and all necessary lists such as indents, etc. from information furnished by employer in the drawings. All drawings prepared by shall be approved by employer’s representative.
RESPONSIBILITY FOR EQUIPMENT

Once the accepts the materials and equipment at the site delivery point in proper condition, the shall be responsible for their safe custody and protection against any loss or damage until final handover of the plant to the Employer. All surplus materials and equipment shall be duly accounted for and handed over to the Employer. The shall maintain proper identification and records of all materials received and installed, to the satisfaction of the Employer’s authorized representative. All materials taken out of the project site shall be allowed only against gate passes issued by authorized personnel of the Employer.
MANUFACTURER'S SPECIAL TOOLS

The will be allowed by employer on case to case basis to make full use of special tools. if any, supplied by the cranes/ hoist’s supplier or equipment. The shall return all such tools to employer on completion of the work in a serviceable condition.
ERECTION, TESTING & COMMISSIOINING:
Erection work involves assembly & alignment of equipment parts including motors and brakes of electrical components and installation of the equipment parts as per approved drawings.

The is to transport all the components from stores / unloading site to the proper location of the erection with their own arrangement. Un-packing of packages and listing of damaged / short supply items to be recorded and immediately reported to Cranes/hoists supplier's site in charge.

To maintain the inventory of items at site, the shall maintain detailed records of all the equipment received and issued from the stores under their control. Unloading and loading of equipment at stores and at erection site shall be the total responsibility of the .
The erection to be done in proper sequence to ease subsequent mounting, alignment, assemblies & subassemblies and safety of man & equipment.
Proper safety measures to be taken as to avoid accidents that may cause damage to material and injury to persons working.

The shall supply all consumable and auxiliary materials like soldering flux, electrodes, insulation materials, PVC tapes and other sundry materials needed for the completion of erection as per specification given by Cranes/hoists supplier.

All Cranes & Hoist structures, mechanical and electrical equipment shall be stored and handled in such a manner that they are not subjected to damage or excessively stressed due to mishandling and / or environmental conditions.
Erection and alignment of electrical items including motors, brakes, electrics items bases.
Initial requirement of lubricant including replenishment during erection and commissioning work shall be informed by the .
To & fro transportation of load for load testing, arranging sling, cradle/platform etc. shall be arranged by the . However, required load for load testing of cranes shall be provided by employer.

The Positioning, leveling and plumbing of the crane girders end carriages, long travel drivers, etc. shall be carried out in the pre-assembly yard in accordance with the approved drawings. For any item of trolley not supplied in assembled condition, the same shall be positioned, leveled and plumbed in the pre -assembly yard in accordance with the approved drawings. The work shall be carried out to the satisfaction of the employer. For the purpose of assembly open space will be provided by the employer subject to availability at site.

The shall carry out the work in accordance with relevant IS, IPSS standards, Indian electricity rules and the drawing data information, instructions furnished by the cranes/hoists’s supplier. The testing and commissioning of the cranes/hoists shall be done in presence of the representatives of the cranes/hoist’s supplier, consultant and the end user.

While conducting tests on the crane/hoist, the shall not be held responsible for any manufacturing short comings of shops but shall be held responsible for defective workmanship carried out during the erection work. The shall arrange slings, wire ropes, stop watches and other necessary equipment/ instrument to carryout the work. The shall be responsible for transporting, loading/ unloading of test loads from other shops of ISP, SAIL to the test site and return them back to the respective shops after the load test is over. The shall also be responsible to stack the test loads suitably for load test.

Acceptance Test parameters to be achieved by the during PG and acceptance test. The erection, alignment & assembly should be such that all the technical parameters as given in the drawing are properly met. Cranes/hoists supplier shall actively help in achieving the test parameters.
PROPOSED ERECTION PROCEDURE:
Erection work involves assembly of equipment parts including electrical components and installation of the equipment parts as per approved drawings.

During erection, the steel work/equipment shall be securely bolted or properly fastened and when necessary shall be temporarily braced to take care of all loads transferred to the structure during erection including those due to erection equipment and its operation.
No permanent bolting or welding should be done until proper alignment has been made & the same is duly inspected & cleared by authorized inspecting agency.
Erection and dismantling of equipment parts shall be carried out in a manner so as to ensure stability of the crane structure at all stages of erection.

The shall be held responsible for the assembly of the equipment parts within the required accuracy. The dimension, of the crane/hoist after assembly shall be within the tolerances as specified. All materials shall be straight unless required to be of curvilinear form and shall be free from twists. All cold straightening shall be done by pressure only.
As far as possible sub-assembly shall carried out on the ground.

Instrumental checking for correctness of alignment of the equipment shall be carried out by the . The necessary instrument shall be arranged by the . Details of alignments done at site will have to be submitted by .

Bolting at site shall be carried out with the same accuracy and care as those adopted in the shop. When parts are to be joined by bolts, these shall be tightened to the maximum limit. The threaded portion of each bolt shall project through the nut by at least one thread. Bolts shall be fitted with spring washer. Where fitted bolts are required, new bolts shall be used with holes re-reamed to the required tolerances.

Welding- Only qualified welders shall be employed to carry out welding process. The welding shall be uniform and free from defects like slag inclusion, blow holes porosity, cracks, under cuts etc. There shall not be over lapping or undercutting of the parent metal.

Necessary adjustment to the structure, miscellaneous rectification of structure, wheels, equipment etc. for satisfactory operation and acceptance of the equipment shall be made by . Any modification, rectification or repairs to members or equipment which have been damaged during transit have to be made good by the to ensure proper assembly of the components. Any modification or repair to members or equipment which have been damaged during storage and handling or otherwise, shall be made good by the free of cost, to ensure proper assembly of the component.

Erection of the equipment and its electrical and mechanical components shall be carried out as per approved erection instructions/documents. Proper care shall be taken so that pipes, electrical wiring etc. are not damaged due to faulty or careless dismantling and erection procedure. The employer decision shall be final regarding any damage due to wrong dismantling or faulty erection procedure and the entire cost of replacement of the damaged part shall be borne by the .

The plan of erection of various motors shall be seen from the relevant diagrams. The equipment may be mounted on base frames. The base frames of all motors shall be laid at the appropriate place provided for the purpose and shall be carefully aligned with the help of leveling instruments and weld properly. Base frames shall also be coated with suitable paints as done for other equipment.
ERECTION SEQUENCE:

may visit the site to see the site condition & to have an idea of similar existing system for better understanding. Keeping in view the overall time schedule, the has to submit the sequence of erection along with realization schedule by the .
TESTS AT SITE BY ERECTION
The cranes/ hoists after erection shall be tested as specified in design basis of Cranes and Hoists (Doc. No. ISPX2-DBR-CH-001, Rev.0)
The crane/hoist shall be offered for inspection and testing during different stages of its erection.
When conducting tests, erector shall provide his own crane operator. However, the employer shall be entitled to employ his own crane operator, if he so desires.
TOUCH UP PAINTING

Before transportation of the equipment from employer’s store to erection site, necessary cleaning, brushing, Shop coats of rust inhibiting paints, lacquers etc. shall be applied to various parts, if necessary.
Damaged and defective shop coats shall be touched up with the same type of paint as used for shop coat.
Requisite quantity of the paints shall be supplied by the cranes/ hoist’s supplier of the equipment.
Before commencing painting of the steel which is delivered unpainted, all surfaces to be painted shall be dry and thoroughly cleaned from all loose mill scales, rust and foreign matters.
After erection the damaged portion occurred due to transport or erection or welding works shall be retouched and then one final coast shall be applied with primer.
Colour scheme shall be as per design basis for painting specification (Doc. No. ISPX2-DBR-PT-001).
SERVICES TO BE PROVIDED BY CRANES/HOISTS SUPPLIER/OEM (EXCLUDED FROM ’S SCOPE)
The following will be in the scope of cranes/hoists supplier/OEM apart from that specifically mentioned against each equipment:
Supply of complete equipment to be erected at Site
Supply of all drawings/documents required for erection, testing & commissioning of the equipment.
Supervision of erection, testing and commissioning and conducting various tests at site as required.
SAFETY PRECAUTIONS DURING ERECTION
General
It will be the principal function of the site in - charge of the to organise a group with qualified engineers primarily with a view to ensure the safety of all concerned people during the erection.
Broad functions of this group are enumerated below:
- Grading of work situations with regard to their propensity for safety.
- Identifying areas of work needing safety measures to be undertaken.
- Devise appropriate safety methodologies for follow - up by all concerned with regard to national codes/ statutory regulations.
- Work out provision of minimum quantum of safety appliances as applicable to a variety of work situations for follow - up.

- Periodically check the working conditions in various erection zones with regard to noise level, ventilation provisions, level of environmental contamination, fire hazards, concentration of heavy erection equipment in work areas, etc. and to recommend/enforce suitable safety measures.
Enforcement of safety regulations on every erection agency
- To ensure that the agencies are aware of the appropriate safety provisions to be made by them.
- To ensure that safety measures are strictly followed.
- To ensure that availability of first aid facilities, along with qualified and trained personnel to administer the same.
- Ensure compliance with national codes/ statutory regulations relating to safety as applicable by all agencies concerned during erection.
- Procurement of various safety appliance, proper knotting of chains, ropes, etc.,
- Periodical checking of all mechanisms and auxiliaries for performance, etc.
- Proper storage of gas cylinders from sources of heat, correct handling of cylinders both during and after use, use of goggles during welding, provision of incombustible screen, etc.
- Clearance of all personnel from work area during shifting of equipment before and after assembly as well as movement.
- Necessary barricading/guarding of areas where critical tests/ erection, etc. may be in progress.
SCOPE OF WORK

children.text: The scope of erection shall include transportation of Auxiliary systems, pipes, pipe fittings and associated items from the purchaser’s stores, erection of auxiliary systems and assemblies such as pumping units, accumulator stands, reservoirs / tanks, hydraulic valve stands, control valves, instruments and piping, structures etc. and interconnecting piping, testing, pickling & flushing, painting, start-up, commissioning and PG test based on PT drawings and documents of all the supplied systems, components and piping network.

metadata.section: 09. TECHNICAL SPECIFICATION FOR ERECTION OF HYDRAULIC, OIL LUBRICATION, GREASE LUBRICATION, DESCALER, AND PNEUMATIC SYSTEMS WITH INTERCONNECTING PIPING & OTHER FACILITIES

children.text: The erection and other activities of the Auxiliary systems shall be carried based on drawings and documents provided by PT duly vetted by Consultant/Client. Following is the general brief scope of work, however any work of erection, supply etc. which is required for completion of erection and commissioning activities of the systems shall also be included in the scope of contractor.

metadata.section: 09. TECHNICAL SPECIFICATION FOR ERECTION OF HYDRAULIC, OIL LUBRICATION, GREASE LUBRICATION, DESCALER, AND PNEUMATIC SYSTEMS WITH INTERCONNECTING PIPING & OTHER FACILITIES

children.text: Each lubrication system shall have Tank (Reservoir) assembly, Pump-motor skid, Filter skid and heat exchanger skid, etc. The contractor shall erect these items on oil cellar / room floor. Foundation bolts shall be hilti bolts. After erection of the individual oil system’s units, the pipes for inter connecting the units shall be fabricated and installed.

metadata.section: 09. TECHNICAL SPECIFICATION FOR ERECTION OF HYDRAULIC, OIL LUBRICATION, GREASE LUBRICATION, DESCALER, AND PNEUMATIC SYSTEMS WITH INTERCONNECTING PIPING & OTHER FACILITIES

children.text: Supply pipe from pumping unit and return piping from the tank unit shall be connected to equipment piping (Interconnecting piping). Piping (piping on mechanical equipment) shall also be completed wherever required. However in some cases there may not be any equipment piping and interconnecting piping shall be terminated with welding etc. up to the consumer point on the equipment.

metadata.section: 09. TECHNICAL SPECIFICATION FOR ERECTION OF HYDRAULIC, OIL LUBRICATION, GREASE LUBRICATION, DESCALER, AND PNEUMATIC SYSTEMS WITH INTERCONNECTING PIPING & OTHER FACILITIES

children.text: These interconnecting piping shall be fabricated and installed in tunnels and trenches and shall include valves and instruments also.

metadata.section: 09. TECHNICAL SPECIFICATION FOR ERECTION OF HYDRAULIC, OIL LUBRICATION, GREASE LUBRICATION, DESCALER, AND PNEUMATIC SYSTEMS WITH INTERCONNECTING PIPING & OTHER FACILITIES

metadata.section: 09. TECHNICAL SPECIFICATION FOR ERECTION OF HYDRAULIC, OIL LUBRICATION, GREASE LUBRICATION, DESCALER, AND PNEUMATIC SYSTEMS WITH INTERCONNECTING PIPING & OTHER FACILITIES

children.text: Each Air oil lubrication system shall be completely piped up unit and shall be directly erected on the oil cellar / room floor. Piping (piping on mechanical equipment) shall also be completed wherever required. Foundation bolts shall be Hilti bolts. Air oil supply pipe shall be connected to the equipment piping. Equipment piping shall not be in the scope of .

metadata.section: 09. TECHNICAL SPECIFICATION FOR ERECTION OF HYDRAULIC, OIL LUBRICATION, GREASE LUBRICATION, DESCALER, AND PNEUMATIC SYSTEMS WITH INTERCONNECTING PIPING & OTHER FACILITIES

metadata.section: 09. TECHNICAL SPECIFICATION FOR ERECTION OF HYDRAULIC, OIL LUBRICATION, GREASE LUBRICATION, DESCALER, AND PNEUMATIC SYSTEMS WITH INTERCONNECTING PIPING & OTHER FACILITIES

children.text: Each hydraulic system shall have tank skid, pump-motor skid, filter skid, Heat exchanger skid and accumulator skid. These skids shall be erected on the oil cellar / room floor. Piping (piping on mechanical equipment) shall also be completed wherever required. The foundation bolts shall be Hilti bolts. After erection of the individual oil system’s units, the pipes for inter connecting the units shall be fabricated and installed.

metadata.section: 09. TECHNICAL SPECIFICATION FOR ERECTION OF HYDRAULIC, OIL LUBRICATION, GREASE LUBRICATION, DESCALER, AND PNEUMATIC SYSTEMS WITH INTERCONNECTING PIPING & OTHER FACILITIES

children.text: Hydraulic valve stands shall be installed near the mechanical equipments on the mill floor or at the ground floor with hilti foundation bolts. Interconnecting piping between pumping unit and valve stand and valve stand and Equipment piping shall be fabricated and installed in tunnels and trenches.

metadata.section: 09. TECHNICAL SPECIFICATION FOR ERECTION OF HYDRAULIC, OIL LUBRICATION, GREASE LUBRICATION, DESCALER, AND PNEUMATIC SYSTEMS WITH INTERCONNECTING PIPING & OTHER FACILITIES

metadata.section: 09. TECHNICAL SPECIFICATION FOR ERECTION OF HYDRAULIC, OIL LUBRICATION, GREASE LUBRICATION, DESCALER, AND PNEUMATIC SYSTEMS WITH INTERCONNECTING PIPING & OTHER FACILITIES

children.text: Valve stands of air control systems shall be mounted on the mill floor with hilti bolts or welded on equipment/ structure as per design requirements. Interconnecting piping between valve stands and air header and between valve stands and equipment piping shall be fabricated and installed in tunnels and trenches. Control valves and instruments requiring air shall be connected by interconnecting piping with shop air headers.

metadata.section: 09. TECHNICAL SPECIFICATION FOR ERECTION OF HYDRAULIC, OIL LUBRICATION, GREASE LUBRICATION, DESCALER, AND PNEUMATIC SYSTEMS WITH INTERCONNECTING PIPING & OTHER FACILITIES

children.text: Automatic centralized grease systems:

metadata.section: 09. TECHNICAL SPECIFICATION FOR ERECTION OF HYDRAULIC, OIL LUBRICATION, GREASE LUBRICATION, DESCALER, AND PNEUMATIC SYSTEMS WITH INTERCONNECTING PIPING & OTHER FACILITIES

children.text: Each centralized dual line grease system shall have pumping unit with reservoir and accessories mounted on a base frame and completely piped up. It shall be installed on the floor with hilti foundation bolts. Interconnecting piping shall be fabricated and installed in trenches to connect the grease pump to the consumer points (At the foot of the equipment) through grease dose feeders. Piping (piping on mechanical equipment) shall also be completed wherever required.

metadata.section: 09. TECHNICAL SPECIFICATION FOR ERECTION OF HYDRAULIC, OIL LUBRICATION, GREASE LUBRICATION, DESCALER, AND PNEUMATIC SYSTEMS WITH INTERCONNECTING PIPING & OTHER FACILITIES

children.text: Descaling system:

metadata.section: 09. TECHNICAL SPECIFICATION FOR ERECTION OF HYDRAULIC, OIL LUBRICATION, GREASE LUBRICATION, DESCALER, AND PNEUMATIC SYSTEMS WITH INTERCONNECTING PIPING & OTHER FACILITIES

children.text: The descaling system shall consist of pumps; each pump shall be mounted on a common base frame and coupled to electric motor with all the accessories. These pumps shall be installed in the descaling room floor with hilti foundation bolts. The erector shall fabricate inter-connecting piping and install the same in tunnels and trenches to connect the pumps to the descaling header.

metadata.section: 09. TECHNICAL SPECIFICATION FOR ERECTION OF HYDRAULIC, OIL LUBRICATION, GREASE LUBRICATION, DESCALER, AND PNEUMATIC SYSTEMS WITH INTERCONNECTING PIPING & OTHER FACILITIES

children.text: The tapping for the service/utility pipes required for the Auxiliary systems shall be taken from the nearest header line near the column / as shown in the drawing. The service / utility pipes shall be installed along the columns and tunnels/trenches. Required valves and instruments shall be installed on these pipes. Pipe supports shall be fabricated by the contractor to support the pipes from the shop columns and civil inserts in tunnels and trenches.

children.text: The Contractor shall provide necessary measures to ensure safe transportation of materials from store to erection site.

children.text: The Contractor shall be responsible for arrangement of adequate material handling equipment like trucks, trailers, forklifts, mobile cranes, tools and tackles as required for this work.

children.text: The necessary lifting tackles, tools, wire ropes, winch blocks, slings of suitable capacities and other equipment necessary to carry out the work shall be arranged by the Contractor. All such lifting tackles should have valid test certificates.

children.text: In case the contractor does not provide truck/trailer as and when required, purchaser can arrange to transport the material through alternate contractor and extra expenditure incurredincluding demurrage, if any will be recovered from the contractor bill.

children.text: The contractor shall be keep a record of all the equipment / items taken by him from the store and shall be responsible for their safe custody after receipt.

children.text: Erection, testing and commissioning of all auxiliary system units / components shall be carried out by the contractor according to latest engineering practices and also as per the approved construction drawings and system schematic drawings.

metadata.section: 09. TECHNICAL SPECIFICATION FOR ERECTION OF HYDRAULIC, OIL LUBRICATION, GREASE LUBRICATION, DESCALER, AND PNEUMATIC SYSTEMS WITH INTERCONNECTING PIPING & OTHER FACILITIES

children.text: The equipment shall be installed on civil foundations. All pits, pockets and sumps shall be thoroughly cleaned by the Contractor. The foundation bases shall be cleaned by water and compressed air to expose the surface. The top of the concrete foundation shall be thoroughly cleaned by the Contractor prior to placement of equipment on foundation. The contractor shall check rough concrete levels, foundation bolt hole locations and levels and pockets for all equipment foundations based on certified civil drawings.

metadata.section: 09. TECHNICAL SPECIFICATION FOR ERECTION OF HYDRAULIC, OIL LUBRICATION, GREASE LUBRICATION, DESCALER, AND PNEUMATIC SYSTEMS WITH INTERCONNECTING PIPING & OTHER FACILITIES

children.text: If any defect on foundation is noticed after placement of equipment on foundation the contractor shall be responsible for taking out the equipment from foundation for rectification and placement of equipment on foundation after rectification. Purchaser shall not be responsible for any idling of labour on account of this during the above period.

metadata.section: 09. TECHNICAL SPECIFICATION FOR ERECTION OF HYDRAULIC, OIL LUBRICATION, GREASE LUBRICATION, DESCALER, AND PNEUMATIC SYSTEMS WITH INTERCONNECTING PIPING & OTHER FACILITIES

children.text: The concrete surface shall be cleaned from all foreign particles and degreased before grouting of the equipment.

metadata.section: 09. TECHNICAL SPECIFICATION FOR ERECTION OF HYDRAULIC, OIL LUBRICATION, GREASE LUBRICATION, DESCALER, AND PNEUMATIC SYSTEMS WITH INTERCONNECTING PIPING & OTHER FACILITIES

children.text: The civil foundation areas below base frame where packing plates would be placed shall be properly chipped and leveled. The auxiliary equipment shall be placed on packing and wedge plates for positioning and leveling. The packing plates are to be leveled by master level.

metadata.section: 09. TECHNICAL SPECIFICATION FOR ERECTION OF HYDRAULIC, OIL LUBRICATION, GREASE LUBRICATION, DESCALER, AND PNEUMATIC SYSTEMS WITH INTERCONNECTING PIPING & OTHER FACILITIES

children.text: All the base frames shall be leveled and pump motor assemblies shall be aligned before giving grouting clearance.

children.text: The contractor shall select and adopt methods and procedures for equipment erection to suit the nature of equipment and erection work involved according to the best modern practice and his own experience in such work. The contractor shall be responsible to provide competent and adequate supervision to ensure that assembly and erection works are in compliance with the equipment manufacturer's drawings/instructions.

children.text: All the foundation bolts shall be Hilti bolts. However at some places their may be requirement of foundation bolts. Hilti bolts and foundation bolts shall be supplied by the purchaser as free issue items. The contractor must have proper machines for drilling holes etc. in the foundations to fix the hilti bolts.

children.text: Pipe routes shall be checked with respect to the pipe system layout, pipeline drawings and foundation drawings. As some of the embedded steel parts may be covered with cement a check shall be made by hammering the wall for identifying locations of embedded steel parts.

metadata.section: 09. TECHNICAL SPECIFICATION FOR ERECTION OF HYDRAULIC, OIL LUBRICATION, GREASE LUBRICATION, DESCALER, AND PNEUMATIC SYSTEMS WITH INTERCONNECTING PIPING & OTHER FACILITIES

children.text: All supports shall be checked for horizontally and verticality before fixing.

metadata.section: 09. TECHNICAL SPECIFICATION FOR ERECTION OF HYDRAULIC, OIL LUBRICATION, GREASE LUBRICATION, DESCALER, AND PNEUMATIC SYSTEMS WITH INTERCONNECTING PIPING & OTHER FACILITIES

children.text: Although embedded steel wall inserts shall be provided by the contractor as per the drawing for welding/fixing of pipe supports, if at any place some inserts are missing and if additional inserts are required, same shall be fixed by the contractor without any commercial implications.

metadata.section: 09. TECHNICAL SPECIFICATION FOR ERECTION OF HYDRAULIC, OIL LUBRICATION, GREASE LUBRICATION, DESCALER, AND PNEUMATIC SYSTEMS WITH INTERCONNECTING PIPING & OTHER FACILITIES

children.text: The contractor shall fabricate suitable structural supports as required depending upon site conditions for proper supporting of all pipelines besides the P.P. clamps envisaged. The holes required for fixing U-clamps shall be drilled and shall not be burnt by gas cutting. However, any deviation shall be complied with the approval of the site engineer only.

metadata.section: 09. TECHNICAL SPECIFICATION FOR ERECTION OF HYDRAULIC, OIL LUBRICATION, GREASE LUBRICATION, DESCALER, AND PNEUMATIC SYSTEMS WITH INTERCONNECTING PIPING & OTHER FACILITIES

children.text: Supports will be so attached that there is no shock/ vibration in any portion of the pipe work.

metadata.section: 09. TECHNICAL SPECIFICATION FOR ERECTION OF HYDRAULIC, OIL LUBRICATION, GREASE LUBRICATION, DESCALER, AND PNEUMATIC SYSTEMS WITH INTERCONNECTING PIPING & OTHER FACILITIES

children.text: Supports will be properly bolted / welded to prevent deformation/slip-off after installation of piping.

metadata.section: 09. TECHNICAL SPECIFICATION FOR ERECTION OF HYDRAULIC, OIL LUBRICATION, GREASE LUBRICATION, DESCALER, AND PNEUMATIC SYSTEMS WITH INTERCONNECTING PIPING & OTHER FACILITIES

children.text: Dust, scale and foreign particles shall be removed by hammering pipes before installation.

metadata.section: 09. TECHNICAL SPECIFICATION FOR ERECTION OF HYDRAULIC, OIL LUBRICATION, GREASE LUBRICATION, DESCALER, AND PNEUMATIC SYSTEMS WITH INTERCONNECTING PIPING & OTHER FACILITIES

children.text: Pipelines will be pre-fabricated to the largest possible extent to minimize field welding.

metadata.section: 09. TECHNICAL SPECIFICATION FOR ERECTION OF HYDRAULIC, OIL LUBRICATION, GREASE LUBRICATION, DESCALER, AND PNEUMATIC SYSTEMS WITH INTERCONNECTING PIPING & OTHER FACILITIES

children.text: Prefabricated pipelines are to be stored properly to avoid deformations.

metadata.section: 09. TECHNICAL SPECIFICATION FOR ERECTION OF HYDRAULIC, OIL LUBRICATION, GREASE LUBRICATION, DESCALER, AND PNEUMATIC SYSTEMS WITH INTERCONNECTING PIPING & OTHER FACILITIES

children.text: Suitable scaffolding is to be provided for laying and installation of pipelines. Pipe works. Equipment and facilities must not be used for this purpose.

metadata.section: 09. TECHNICAL SPECIFICATION FOR ERECTION OF HYDRAULIC, OIL LUBRICATION, GREASE LUBRICATION, DESCALER, AND PNEUMATIC SYSTEMS WITH INTERCONNECTING PIPING & OTHER FACILITIES

children.text: All pipe work including pipe fittings shall be cleaned of oil, grease, foreign matter, rust, scale, dirt etc. by wire brushing and wiping prior to start of fabrication work. The fabrication work shall be carried out in clean area and with approved procedures.

metadata.section: 09. TECHNICAL SPECIFICATION FOR ERECTION OF HYDRAULIC, OIL LUBRICATION, GREASE LUBRICATION, DESCALER, AND PNEUMATIC SYSTEMS WITH INTERCONNECTING PIPING & OTHER FACILITIES

children.text: Pipelines will be connected to mechanical equipment / piping on equipment after final alignment and leveling thereof. The connections shall be free form stresses.

metadata.section: 09. TECHNICAL SPECIFICATION FOR ERECTION OF HYDRAULIC, OIL LUBRICATION, GREASE LUBRICATION, DESCALER, AND PNEUMATIC SYSTEMS WITH INTERCONNECTING PIPING & OTHER FACILITIES

children.text: Piping close to equipment shall be installed in such a way that dismantling and inspection of the equipment will not be hindered.

metadata.section: 09. TECHNICAL SPECIFICATION FOR ERECTION OF HYDRAULIC, OIL LUBRICATION, GREASE LUBRICATION, DESCALER, AND PNEUMATIC SYSTEMS WITH INTERCONNECTING PIPING & OTHER FACILITIES

children.text: The service piping like water, air etc shall be done from Purchaser's design limit to the equipment as per drawings.

metadata.section: 09. TECHNICAL SPECIFICATION FOR ERECTION OF HYDRAULIC, OIL LUBRICATION, GREASE LUBRICATION, DESCALER, AND PNEUMATIC SYSTEMS WITH INTERCONNECTING PIPING & OTHER FACILITIES

children.text: Alll pipeline erection shall be suitably routed through tunnel / trenches, mounted on machine structures as indicated in approved drawings and shall be aligned by spirit level/plumb or as per instruction of site engineer. All pipe work shall have a good aesthetic look for purchaser's satisfaction.

metadata.section: 09. TECHNICAL SPECIFICATION FOR ERECTION OF HYDRAULIC, OIL LUBRICATION, GREASE LUBRICATION, DESCALER, AND PNEUMATIC SYSTEMS WITH INTERCONNECTING PIPING & OTHER FACILITIES

children.text: During welding of pipes having carbon content less than 0.25 %, for pipes 20 mm and below, minimum temperature of the parent metal shall not be less than 5 degree C and for pipes above 20 mm, minimum temperature of parent metal shall not be less than 200 degree C. This minimum temperature shall be maintained through out the welding period on a particular welding joint. However there shall be no pipe with thickness of 20 mm and above.

metadata.section: 09. TECHNICAL SPECIFICATION FOR ERECTION OF HYDRAULIC, OIL LUBRICATION, GREASE LUBRICATION, DESCALER, AND PNEUMATIC SYSTEMS WITH INTERCONNECTING PIPING & OTHER FACILITIES

children.text: Welding joints shall be sound and free of any welding defects. Welding consumables for any fabrication work shall be of approved make and quality. Weld-edge preparation, type of welding, position of welding, cleaning of welding slag between passes, marking and weld profile etc shall be as per approved welding practice. Any welding joint, on visual inspection, if found not conforming to the requirement shall be liable for rejection. The contractor shall thereupon take necessary corrective measures to ensure proper quality. The contractor shall make necessary arrangement and provide facilities for carrying out tests for welding defects. Edge preparation for welding joints shall be done as per ANSI- B-16.9-1993. For Hydraulic, descaling and Circulating Oil Systems (All seamless pipes) butt welding joints root run shall be done by Tungsten Inert Gas (TIG) welding and filling shall be done by electric arc welding. Butt weld of all SS pipes shall also have root run by TIG welding and filling by electric arc welding. Pipes 50 NB and below shall be joined by socket weld fittings.

metadata.section: 09. TECHNICAL SPECIFICATION FOR ERECTION OF HYDRAULIC, OIL LUBRICATION, GREASE LUBRICATION, DESCALER, AND PNEUMATIC SYSTEMS WITH INTERCONNECTING PIPING & OTHER FACILITIES

Actual inside dia at any portion of the bend shall not deviate by more than allowed. Hot bending shall not be allowed.

Duration of test shall be minimum 15 min.

metadata.section: 09. TECHNICAL SPECIFICATION FOR ERECTION OF HYDRAULIC, OIL LUBRICATION, GREASE LUBRICATION, DESCALER, AND PNEUMATIC SYSTEMS WITH INTERCONNECTING PIPING & OTHER FACILITIES

children.text: The contractor shall deploy experienced and resourceful sub contractor exclusively for pickling and flushing of hydraulics and lubricating oil pipe. Before start of pickling and flushing, the contractor shall supply Pickling and flushing procedure to the purchaser for approval and shall undertake pickling and flushing as per the approved procedure. If there is any procedure provided by the PT, the same shall be implemented for pickling and flushing of pipelines. All the hydraulic, oil lubrication and in some cases grease lubrication piping shall be INSITU pickled and flushed. In addition to pickling and flushing procedure, for final INSITU cleaning / flushing for other pipes such as water and air pipes not requiring pickling and flushing shall be provided by the contractor for approval of purchaser. The approved procedure shall be followed by the contractor for cleaning and flushing of water and air pipelines.

metadata.section: 09. TECHNICAL SPECIFICATION FOR ERECTION OF HYDRAULIC, OIL LUBRICATION, GREASE LUBRICATION, DESCALER, AND PNEUMATIC SYSTEMS WITH INTERCONNECTING PIPING & OTHER FACILITIES

children.text: The piping contractor shall supply enough tanks, pumps for pickling, flushing skids consisting of pumps, hose connections, heating facilities, filters of different ratings, wire mesh sheets, valves and small hammers to permit processing of two or more systems simultaneously as required. The wire mesh sheets of adequate mesh size for pickling and flushing shall be supplied by the contractor. However, filter elements for final flushing through system filters shall be supplied by the PT as free issue items to the contractor. Tank / bath pickling of smaller lengths if any removed from the piping net work shall be done while the system is being pickled by circulation.

For timely completion of all the activities, the contractor shall deploy additional man power as and when required. The Contractor shall deploy all labour - skilled, semi-skilled and unskilled, riggers, mill wrights, certified welders, pipe fitters, instrument fitters, mechanics, licensed electricians, supervisors, foremen, erection and testing engineers etc. needed for proper erection and commissioning of the plant and equipment. children.text: The trays shall be earthed and rendered electrically continuous by welding the trays to the grounding strip at not less than two places from both sides of the tray. The earthing strip shall be joined with the main earthing grid at least at two points. This shall be a part of cable tray erection.

metadata.section: 09. TECHNICAL SPECIFICATION FOR ERECTION OF HYDRAULIC, OIL LUBRICATION, GREASE LUBRICATION, DESCALER, AND PNEUMATIC SYSTEMS WITH INTERCONNECTING PIPING & OTHER FACILITIES

children.text: In some cases cables have to be laid through GI pipes. The has to lay the GI pipes and pull boxes. Conduits and Pull boxes must be connected to the earth grid. ’s scope includes laying of pipes/conduits, providing required bends as per site requirement. Sharp open end of the conduit has to be made smooth by filing or any other means to avoid damage of the cable sheath. End of conduits should be blocked / sealed. Conduits must be suitably clamped using saddle clamps at intervals of roughly 1 meter.

metadata.section: 09. TECHNICAL SPECIFICATION FOR ERECTION OF HYDRAULIC, OIL LUBRICATION, GREASE LUBRICATION, DESCALER, AND PNEUMATIC SYSTEMS WITH INTERCONNECTING PIPING & OTHER FACILITIES

children.text: Conduits/GI pipes shall be bent using conduit bending machines. to arrange for proper bending machines at site on 24 hour basis as well as dies for threading conduits of diameters and threads corresponding to the standards. Bends shall be free from cracks, crimps or other damage to the pipe or its coating. This shall be included in the scope of erection of GI pipes/conduits and no separate rate or additional charge shall be entertained for this.

metadata.section: 09. TECHNICAL SPECIFICATION FOR ERECTION OF HYDRAULIC, OIL LUBRICATION, GREASE LUBRICATION, DESCALER, AND PNEUMATIC SYSTEMS WITH INTERCONNECTING PIPING & OTHER FACILITIES

children.text: Ends of conduits shall be suitably grinded to remove rough edges. The threaded ends of conduits shall be painted with anticorrosive paint. The outer ends shall be smoothened free of burrs and sharp edges.

metadata.section: 09. TECHNICAL SPECIFICATION FOR ERECTION OF HYDRAULIC, OIL LUBRICATION, GREASE LUBRICATION, DESCALER, AND PNEUMATIC SYSTEMS WITH INTERCONNECTING PIPING & OTHER FACILITIES

children.text: Wire earthing shall be provided along the entire length of the conduits. Conduits shall be joined by threading and using joining sockets or conduit boxes or pull boxes. For this purpose, must have conduit bending machine and threading tools at site. Welding shall not be allowed for conduit jointing.

metadata.section: 09. TECHNICAL SPECIFICATION FOR ERECTION OF HYDRAULIC, OIL LUBRICATION, GREASE LUBRICATION, DESCALER, AND PNEUMATIC SYSTEMS WITH INTERCONNECTING PIPING & OTHER FACILITIES

children.text: Earthing terminals shall be provided at each conduit box.(except Fiber Optic & Bus Cables).

metadata.section: 09. TECHNICAL SPECIFICATION FOR ERECTION OF HYDRAULIC, OIL LUBRICATION, GREASE LUBRICATION, DESCALER, AND PNEUMATIC SYSTEMS WITH INTERCONNECTING PIPING & OTHER FACILITIES

children.text: All saddles, tubes and boxes shall be in perfect alignment to avoid any appearance of warping when the installation is complete. Saddles should not be so tight as to prevent expansion of the conduit. Steel conduits running on the surface shall be secured with galvanised spacer bar or distance type saddles. Saddles shall be spaced not more than 2m apart.

metadata.section: 09. TECHNICAL SPECIFICATION FOR ERECTION OF HYDRAULIC, OIL LUBRICATION, GREASE LUBRICATION, DESCALER, AND PNEUMATIC SYSTEMS WITH INTERCONNECTING PIPING & OTHER FACILITIES

children.text: Particular care shall be taken to ensure that no foreign material enters the conduit through joints or through surface openings. Jointing of conduits shall be done only in straight portion and not in bend portion. Screw holes in conduit boxes and the like must be kept entirely free and clean. Empty conduits left for future use shall be capped. At conduit terminations at plain holes in metal enclosures or in trunking, flanged couplers shall be used.

metadata.section: 09. TECHNICAL SPECIFICATION FOR ERECTION OF HYDRAULIC, OIL LUBRICATION, GREASE LUBRICATION, DESCALER, AND PNEUMATIC SYSTEMS WITH INTERCONNECTING PIPING & OTHER FACILITIES

children.text: Conduits buried in screed shall be installed without joints over the length of the conduit in the screed. Where this is not possible the shall ensure that the joints are adequately sealed without the conductivity of the conduit being impaired. Joints shall be painted with bituminous based paint.

metadata.section: 09. TECHNICAL SPECIFICATION FOR ERECTION OF HYDRAULIC, OIL LUBRICATION, GREASE LUBRICATION, DESCALER, AND PNEUMATIC SYSTEMS WITH INTERCONNECTING PIPING & OTHER FACILITIES

children.text: Flexible conduits shall be used during the final run of the cables being connected to the motors/ field devices etc. Flexible conduits must be fixed using end sockets and clamps. Flexible conduits shall be of heavy duty PVC coated type.

metadata.section: 09. TECHNICAL SPECIFICATION FOR ERECTION OF HYDRAULIC, OIL LUBRICATION, GREASE LUBRICATION, DESCALER, AND PNEUMATIC SYSTEMS WITH INTERCONNECTING PIPING & OTHER FACILITIES

Minor Chipping and Grouting of such supports are in the scope of the . Wherever felt necessary by PURCHASER, anchor bolts may be used. Fixing of anchor bolts shall being the scope of the . children.text: ’s scope of work shall include making mounting provision and installation of instrumentation & control equipment; construction of all civil/ structural rooms along with necessary facilities for instrumentation & controls; fabrication & erection of maintenance platform & approach ladder and fabrication & erection of structures required for cable laying for instrumentation & control system. Detailed requirement of these rooms is indicated in clause no. 10.09.4.

metadata.section: 11 TECHNICAL SPECIFICATION FOR ERECTION WORKS OF INSTRUMENTATION & CONTROL (For Re-Heating Furnace)

children.text: ’s scope of work shall also include receipt of free issue items (instruments) and erection hardware (impulse pipes & tubes, impulse pipe fittings required for installation of instruments, electrical accessories, panels / cabinets and all associated hardware), transportation to site including loading, unloading, temporary storage of all free issue items; erection, testing (including Hydro testing of pressure tapping point) and commissioning of the instruments and control items. ’s scope shall include temporary storage of free issue items with watch and ward, distribution of construction power, temporary lighting required for erection work. During erection, instrumentation equipment will be handed over to the , time to time, as per the progress of work. The scope of work shall also include construction of site laboratory for testing & calibration of instruments before installation as well as during testing & commissioning of the plant, if required due to complain from client/ consultant/ technological pkg. contractor for any malfunctioning. ’s scope shall also include liquidation of defect points, participation in tests for establishment of plant performance guarantee (PG) and post commissioning activities till issue of final acceptance certificate (FAC) by Purchaser.

This office-cum-laboratory will have brick walled & ventilated room(s) with suitable storage racks, cub boards/ almirahs, etc. for safekeeping of instruments, accessories & fittings, etc. along with semi-covered & fenced compound walls for safe keeping of steel structures, trays, cables (issued) etc. Porta cabin can be used instead of brick walled rooms. Porta cabins shall be taken back by . All documents, gate entry etc. shall be prepared accordingly by . All the erection work at site, including installation of instruments, making tapping point arrangement, impulse piping, transmitter cabinets, structurers for cable laying, maintenance platform & approach ladder erection, etc. will be inspected by representatives of Client/Consultant/ respective technological pkg. contractor. ’s responsibility shall include strict compliance with these requirements and satisfactory implementation of all recommendation of these agencies to ensure completion of work in conformity with plans, specifications and standards.

shall maintain proper records for receipt and issue of materials/items. shall prepare & submit Reconciliation reports of all the items, materials issued/used by him after successful completion & issue of FAC of this project.
The erection, testing and commissioning activities of instrumentation equipment shall be carried out by competent & qualified technical persons of the .

shall work in close co-ordination with Balance Of Plant (BOP) pkg. contractor (who will supply and erect the cables, cable trays and Junction boxes) and other agencies working at the same or nearby units at the same time on different activities involved in this project to ensure smooth progress of erection, testing & commissioning work and without causing disturbances & delays in each other’s work.

shall deploy required number of Supervisory, Skilled, Unskilled and Auxiliary personnel including welders, gas cutters, instrument fitters, riggers, mechanics, licensed electricians, foremen, supervisors, engineers, resident engineer, senior engineer, etc., as may be required for complete erection, testing, preliminary acceptance, commissioning, performance guarantee, final acceptance & defects liability periodasdefined in this TS and comply with the instructions of Client/ Consultant/ respective technological pkg. contractor, in the interest of satisfactory progress and completion of the work according to the schedule. shall suitably augment his manpower, as and when required, work on 3 shifts per day basis, if & wherever required, to meet the schedules/ requirements without any contractual implications. List of all categories of manpower to be deployed along with their credentials and experience shall be submitted by the .
Besides augmenting the manpower, shall also fabricate all required stanchions, trays etc. and installed/ erected all possible instrumentation items & accessories.
shall also fabricate all required expander & reducer required to install online flow meters, control valves, wherever the size of these will be lower than the pipe line size.

shall construct his site office-cum-laboratory equipped with all facilities as per requirements indicated in this TS and also mobilise his resources, manpower, supply of erection material, hardware, tools & tackles, testing & calibrating equipment, etc. well in advance to ensure that erection activities can start fromstarting date/ intimation by Client/ Consultant. shall be also responsible for getting Preliminary Acceptance Certificate (PAC) & Final Acceptance Certificate (FAC) from Client.

shall note that monitoring of site progress & corresponding invoicing will be based on satisfactory completion of erection of respective instrumentation equipment/ item. For example, erection of a ‘Pressure Transmitter’ will be considered complete & legible for raising invoice only after its pre-installation calibration checks, installation on stanchion/stand pipe; supply, laying & connecting of impulse piping/ pneumatic tubing with required fittings & accessories as per installation diagram are carried out satisfactorily. Similarly, progress of other activities/ work shall be accounted. The formats indicated in schedule of quantities shall properly fill-up and submitted with the offer by .

shall perform erection jobs for all the instrumentation equipment indicated at bill of materials. shall also quote unit price for these erection jobs w.r.t instrumentation equipment indicated in schedule of quantities, with validity of all the unit rates till final acceptance. CIVIL JOBS FOR INSTRUMENTATION & CONTROL SYSTEM.
For Instrumentation & control system, two types of room shall be constructed, viz. Analyser rooms and Instrument laboratory cum area repair shop.
Analyzer rooms
The Analyser Room may please be constructed with facilities as indicated below:
Room shall be provided with metallic entry door.
The Analyser Room shall have standard double-walled sliding glass windows (2 nos. of size 2000(W)x 1300(H), one at front side & other at back side of room), open-able & lockable type.
The analyser room will have industrial grade package type air-conditioning system. The ACVS is under BOP package vendor’s scope. Hence, AHU room shall be provided at the side of each analyser room.

The gas cylinders shall be planned to kept outside the room. The Cylinder shall be open to atmosphere and under shed. The size of the shed shall be considered as 2500(L)x750(W). Also, grill shall be provided with lock & key arrangement, to place the gas cylinder in a secure place.

Depth & width of the trench shall be considered as 500mm. The trench shall be provided in the room from one wall to another wall. Cables shall be laid through trench. MS insert plate of size 100x100x8 mm @500mm shall be provided (100 mm above bottom of trench) at both sides of trench for laying of cable trays. For fixing of cable trays, angles (50x50x6 mm) shall be fixed with the above referred insert plates. Around 50% of the trench shall be covered with removable type MS chequered plates.

Necessary opening, plate inserts, pipes etc. shall be provided for entry of sample gas lines, purging air/ nitrogen line, test gas line, venting gas line and for cable entry. Following pipe inserts shall be considered as minimum:
The analyser panel room will be provided with exhaust fan with louver. Necessary opening (12”) shall be kept for installation of the same.

children.text: An isolation valve shall be provided in the impulse line before transmitter. This isolation valve shall be in addition to the first root valve and valve manifold. Drain/vent provision of impulse lines shall be provided with drain/vent valve. The drain/ vent point of valve shall be plugged or the extended drain/vent point shall be caped.

children.text: Corrosive media shall be separated from transmitters by separating chambers using seal pot arrangement, for non-diaphragm seal type transmitters. For congealing service also, seal pot arrangement shall be provided, for non-diaphragm seal type transmitters.

metadata.section: 2 nos., 50 mm dia. Medium grade GI pipe, at elevation +1300mm from FFL (above the gas cylinder mounting location), for entering calibration gas line.

children.text: For dirty/ contaminated gas applications, if required, continuous N2/ compressed air purging through rotameters and/ or intermittent purging through remote operated solenoid valve in each impulse line shall be provided. Y tap shall be used for poking of taping point. Poking leg of the Y tap shall be of minimum 50 mm dia.

children.text: For steam or vapour at temperature greater than 120C, condensation chamber shall be used. Condensation chambers shall be installed as near to tapping point as possible. Condensation chambers shall be IBR certified for steam application, which comes under IBR guideline.

children.text: All welding shall be carried out as per welding procedures and codes with electrodes approved by client. Only qualified welders shall carry out welding.

children.text: For installation of impulse tubes with compression fittings, proper care shall be taken for handling, bending of the tubes and tightening of the fittings. Standard instruction of the manufacturer shall be followed strictly for the above cases.

metadata.section: 2 nos., 50 mm dia. Medium grade GI pipe, at elevation +1300mm from FFL (above the gas cylinder mounting location), for entering calibration gas line.

children.text: Pipes/tubes shall be bent using pipe/tube benders only. Hot bending of impulse pipes/tubes is prohibited. Impulse line bending shall be circular and smooth. Flat bending shall be rejected. Pipes/ tubes shall be cut using pipe cutting device. Hot cutting will not be allowed.

metadata.section: 2 nos., 50 mm dia. Medium grade GI pipe, at elevation +1300mm from FFL (above the gas cylinder mounting location), for entering calibration gas line.

children.text: All threaded joints shall be joined with Teflon tape and no other joining component shall be used except on high temperature service, where graphite sealing components shall be used. Pipe threading shall be made using suitable threading machine.

children.text: Impulse lines shall be properly supported and shall be clamped with the support. Impulse lines shall never be welded with the support, metal constructions, equipment or structure of building. Supports shall not generally be taken from process pipes, equipment, handrails etc.

children.text: Proper slope in impulse line (minimum 1:10) shall be maintained.

children.text: Air supply piping shall be installed with a slope of not less than 1/100 in required direction to prevent accumulation of condensed water within the pipe. Also, some drain point arrangement shall be made on air pipeline, as per the piping drg.

children.text: Specification for welding of piping

metadata.section: 2 nos., 50 mm dia. Medium grade GI pipe, at elevation +1300mm from FFL (above the gas cylinder mounting location), for entering calibration gas line.

children.text: Components to be welded shall be properly aligned, spaced and fitted up so as to hold the edges during welding at proper distance to obtain full root penetration. Any misalignment between the edges to be welded shall not exceed 1.5 mm, and care shall be taken to distribute it along the circumference. Should the misalignment be over 1.5 mm, the end of the pipe with the smaller ID shall be internally machines or ground, so as to reduce the misalignment to the prescribed value. The machined area shall be tapered with a slope not greater than1:3.

metadata.section: 2 nos., 50 mm dia. Medium grade GI pipe, at elevation +1300mm from FFL (above the gas cylinder mounting location), for entering calibration gas line.

children.text: Any surface irregularities shall be grounded off and filed.

metadata.section: 2 nos., 50 mm dia. Medium grade GI pipe, at elevation +1300mm from FFL (above the gas cylinder mounting location), for entering calibration gas line.

children.text: For fillet welds (slip-on flanges, socket welds, etc) tack welding shall be permitted, using the electrodes allowed for the weld execution, with preheating when required for the pipe welds. The tacks shall be removed or molten during welding. In the latter case, the tacks shall be checked to ensure that they are free from defects. In socket welds, a gap of 2 mm between the end of the pipe and the socket bottom shall be provided.

children.text: The instruments envisaged to be tested and calibrated in ’s site lab. are: Pressure & Differential Pressure Transmitters; Flow Transmitters (DPT); Level Transmitters (DPT), Temperature Elements (RTDs & Thermocouples); Pressure/ differential pressure switches (set point checking); Pressure gauges, Temperature Gauges, Temperature Transmitters; Control valves with actuators, E/P positioners & other accessories (100 % for all).

children.text: The erection work shall be carried out according to the sound established engineering practice and as per the drawings and specifications supplied by the Purchaser. The Successful shall carry out the work in the presence and / or as per the instructions of the site engineer/ supervisory personnel deputed by the Purchaser. The erection work shall be carried out by highly skilled workforce. The Successful shall not remove any engineer, erector or skilled labour from the site without prior written approval of the Purchaser.
