children.text: Radius of smooth bend of pipe shall be at least 3 times the pipe dia unless otherwise specified. Radius of other fabricated bends of pipes shall be 1.5 times the pipe dia.

children.text: Pipe sealant shall be used to make up screwed joints and shall be applied to male threads only. Threads shall be wiped clean of oil or grease using appropriate solvent if necessary and allowing proper time for drying before applying the sealant ( Jute&Primar / Teflon tape). Pipe shall be free from fins and burrs. Pipe ends shall be reamed or filled out to size of bore and all chips shall be removed.

children.text: Following steps shall be taken by the Successful , besides controlling quality in general, to make effective control in carrying out welded joints.

metadata.section: 04.02.04 Method of Quality Control in Welded Joints

children.text: Welding procedures shall be prepared in line with IS:7307(Part-I)-1974, reaffirmed in 1991 and tests shall be carried out to quality procedures Number of procedures will depend on variables like positions of welding, thickness range etc. Once a welding procedure is qualified, strict adherence to it shall be made during actual welding.

metadata.section: 04.02.04 Method of Quality Control in Welded Joints

children.text: Welders employed shall be qualified as per IS:7310 (Part-I)-1974, reaffirmed in 1987 after passing necessary tests.

metadata.section: 04.02.04 Method of Quality Control in Welded Joints

children.text: Welding consumables shall be of approved type. Such consumables are, however, subject to qualifying initial check tests as per IS: 814-1991.

metadata.section: 04.02.04 Method of Quality Control in Welded Joints

children.text: The Successful shall strictly follow approved welding procedures during actual welding. Besides, the following stages of inspection shall also be carried out :

metadata.section: 04.02.04 Method of Quality Control in Welded Joints

children.text: Weld edge preparation before welding, proper fit-up, position of welding, cleaning of slag between the passes, proper weld profile etc., shall be checked visually, amongst others.

metadata.section: 04.02.04 Method of Quality Control in Welded Joints

children.text: ii) Non-destructive tests of weld of welded joints shall be carried out as per approved procedures/ drawings.

metadata.section: 04.02.04 Method of Quality Control in Welded Joints

children.text: The Successful shall prepare the edge correctly to the shape, size and dimensions of the V-grooves as per ANSI B-16.25 unless otherwise specified in drawings / documents.

metadata.section: 04.02.04 Method of Quality Control in Welded Joints

children.text: The welding surfaces shall be smooth, uniform and free from fins, teers, notches or any other defects which may adversely affect welding and shall be free from loose scale, slag, rust, grease, paint, moisture or any other foreign material. The clean surface shall extend to about 10 mm beyond the welding faces to avoid contamination of the weld metal with foreign material and to avoid unsound weld deposit.

metadata.section: 04.02.04 Method of Quality Control in Welded Joints

children.text: Assembly, Alignment& Welding

metadata.section: 04.02.04 Method of Quality Control in Welded Joints

children.text: The sequence of welding shall be so planned and followed that there shall be a balance of welding about the neutral axis of the fabrication. The Successful shall employ sufficient number of welders working at the same time in diagonal quadrants of the shell using back-step method of welding. The rate of progress of each welder shall be more or less equal and quality uniform.

metadata.section: 04.02.04 Method of Quality Control in Welded Joints

children.text: The general direction of welding shall be towards the free end of the joint, but in a long joint as in shell fabrication, back-step technique shall be used to reduce distortion.

metadata.section: 04.02.04 Method of Quality Control in Welded Joints

children.text: To minimise internal locked up stresses due to welding, the vertical joints for the shell shall be welded and completed to a circular course and then the horizontal circumferential seam welded. However, before the welding of horizontal seam started, the complete circular course shall be aligned and adjusted for their correct axes.

metadata.section: 04.02.04 Method of Quality Control in Welded Joints

children.text: Alternatively, the Successful shall complete each course in all respects on ground and then the pre-assembled course shall be listed and placed in position. In such a sequence and planning, the same principle of balancing of weld about the neutral axis shall be followed and the method of fabrication of each course shall be similar to fabrication of penstock pipes or transmission/industrial pipelines of bigger diameter.

metadata.section: 04.02.04 Method of Quality Control in Welded Joints

children.text: Pipe and attachment shall be aligned properly by accurate and permanent methods prior to welding. If tack welds are used, the tack shall be either fused into the first layer or weld or else chipped out.

metadata.section: 04.02.04 Method of Quality Control in Welded Joints

children.text: Tests on Welded Joints

metadata.section: 04.02.04 Method of Quality Control in Welded Joints

children.text: (a) Visual Examination

metadata.section: 04.02.04 Method of Quality Control in Welded Joints

children.text: The Successful shall conduct visual examination and measurement of external dimensions of the weld for all joints. Before examining the welded joints, areas close to it on both sides of the weld for a width not less than 20 mm shall be cleared of slag and other impurities. Examination shall be done by a magnifying glass which has a magnification power of ten (10) and measuring instrument which has an accuracy of + 0.1 mm or by weld gauges. Welded joints shall be examined from both sides. The Successful shall examine the following during the visual examination :

metadata.section: 04.02.04 Method of Quality Control in Welded Joints

children.text: i) Incomplete penetration of weld metal.

metadata.section: 04.02.04 Method of Quality Control in Welded Joints

children.text: ii) Influx.

metadata.section: 04.02.04 Method of Quality Control in Welded Joints

children.text: iii) Burns.

metadata.section: 04.02.04 Method of Quality Control in Welded Joints

children.text: v) Under cuts.

metadata.section: 04.02.04 Method of Quality Control in Welded Joints

children.text: vi) Unwelded crates.

metadata.section: 04.02.04 Method of Quality Control in Welded Joints

children.text: vii) Cracks in welded spots and heat affected zones.

metadata.section: 04.02.04 Method of Quality Control in Welded Joints

children.text: viii) Porosity in welds and spot welds.

metadata.section: 04.02.04 Method of Quality Control in Welded Joints

children.text: ix) Compression in welded joints as a result of electrode impact while carrying out contact welding.

metadata.section: 04.02.04 Method of Quality Control in Welded Joints

children.text: x) Displacement of welded element.

metadata.section: 04.02.04 Method of Quality Control in Welded Joints

children.text: The Successful shall carryout any or all tests as specified hereinafter to satisfy the Purchaser about the acceptability of the welded joints, as directed by the Purchaser.

metadata.section: 04.02.04 Method of Quality Control in Welded Joints

children.text: (b) Liquid Dye-penetration Test

metadata.section: 04.02.04 Method of Quality Control in Welded Joints

children.text: Liquid dye-penetration tests shall be carried out in accordance with IS:3658-1981, reaffirmed in 1991 for surface/sub-surface defects.

metadata.section: 04.02.04 Method of Quality Control in Welded Joints

children.text: (c) Test by Blowing

metadata.section: 04.02.04 Method of Quality Control in Welded Joints

children.text: The surface of the weld shall be covered with soap solution consisting of one 1 litre of water and 100 gram of soap. Compressed air shall be blown from the opposite side at pressure of 4 to 5 kg/cm2. The distance between the tip of base and the weld shall not be more than 50 mm. Any formation of soap bubble will indicate welding defects. Portable compressor shall be arranged by the Successful , if necessary, for this purpose.

metadata.section: 04.02.04 Method of Quality Control in Welded Joints

children.text: Underground Pipelines

metadata.section: 04.02.04 Method of Quality Control in Welded Joints

children.text: Excavation of Trenches

metadata.section: 04.02.04 Method of Quality Control in Welded Joints

children.text: Trench excavation shall include the removal of all materials including the hauling of excess material to disposal area and usable material to a nearby stockpile area. Excavation in both common and rock material shall be carried out to the lines and grades shown on the drawings unless otherwise specified.

metadata.section: 04.02.04 Method of Quality Control in Welded Joints

children.text: Depth of excavation shall be such that a minimum earth cover as per drawing above the top of the pipeline and 150 mm below the bottom of the pipeline for sand cushioning are ensured. Average bottom width of the trench shall be 600 mm more than outside diameter of the pipe and the side slope shall be maintained to suit the nature of the soil to preclude collapse of the sides.

metadata.section: 04.02.04 Method of Quality Control in Welded Joints

children.text: In the event that trench is constructed to a width or depth greater than that permitted above, through Successful ’s error, the Successful shall furnish the necessary material, labour and equipment to correct such error as decided by the Purchaser/Consultant.

metadata.section: 04.02.04 Method of Quality Control in Welded Joints

children.text: Shoring and bracing shall be used to support the sides of trenches wherever necessary to protect life and limbs of workers. When closed sheeting is required, it shall be so driven as to prevent adjacent soil from entering trenches either below or through sheeting and bracing shall be removed after they have served their purpose.

metadata.section: 04.02.04 Method of Quality Control in Welded Joints

children.text: Any rock, lump of soil, roots or other objects evident on the trench floor, as well as rocks or rock fragments evident within the trench walls, which could or prove detrimental the pipe protective coating, shall be removed.

metadata.section: 04.02.04 Method of Quality Control in Welded Joints

children.text: The Successful shall ensure that water is not allowed to accumulate in open trenches where work is in an incomplete stage. Where necessary, the trench shall be kept dry by pumping out seepage or stray water from any other source continuously.

metadata.section: 04.02.04 Method of Quality Control in Welded Joints

children.text: The trench shall be excavated and maintained by the Successful until the pipe is laid. If the trench wall collapses or the trench suffers damage for whatsoever reason, the Successful shall re-trench as necessary at his own cost.

metadata.section: 04.02.04 Method of Quality Control in Welded Joints

children.text: The trench bottom shall be uniformly graded to proper depth so as to offer a continuous and uniform bearing surface for pipe all along the trench.

metadata.section: 04.02.04 Method of Quality Control in Welded Joints

children.text: Disposal

metadata.section: 04.02.04 Method of Quality Control in Welded Joints

children.text: Excavated materials deemed suitable by the Purchaser/ Consultant for any purpose shall be stockpiled in required quantities in the vicinity of the excavation sites involved, as per the approval / directions of the Purchaser / Consultant, up to a lead of 50 RM. Unsuitable or excess excavation material and debris shall be transported to dump areas up to a lead of 3Km as directed and approved by the Purchaser / Consultant. Material shall be end-dumped and graded at these areas.

metadata.section: 04.02.04 Method of Quality Control in Welded Joints

children.text: Backfill

metadata.section: 04.02.04 Method of Quality Control in Welded Joints

children.text: Backfill shall contain no ashes, rubbish, combustible or decomposable material, nor any other material which the Purchaser/Consultant deem unsuitable for this purpose. If the excavated material is deemed unsuitable by the Purchaser / Consultant for use as backfill, the Successful shall backfill with material approved by the Purchaser/Consultant which shall be obtained from locations to be specified.

metadata.section: 04.02.04 Method of Quality Control in Welded Joints

children.text: Prior to the lowering and laying of pipe in any trench or excavation, the Successful shall provide sand bedding of minimum 150 mm below the pipe and compact the bedding with a light hand hammer. For the purpose of bedding only screened fine sand of grain size not larger than 2 mm shall be used. The sand shall be clean and free from clay lumps, injurious amounts of dust, organic matter, loam or other deleterious substances.

metadata.section: 04.02.04 Method of Quality Control in Welded Joints

children.text: Backfilling shall be conducted at all times in such a manner as to prevent damage and abrasion to protection coating on the pipe.

metadata.section: 04.02.04 Method of Quality Control in Welded Joints

children.text: Placing of backfill about the protected pipe shall be done after conducting necessary tests in the presence of the Purchaser/Consultant and after his final inspection and acceptance.

metadata.section: 04.02.04 Method of Quality Control in Welded Joints

children.text: Only soft earth and murrum of good quality free from stones, roots, vegetation, etc. shall be used for filling in around the pipes for at least 300 mm all around pipes. Filling shall be done in layers not exceeding 150 mm thick and compacted to 95% of the maximum dry density as per Part VII of IS:2720. The remaining portion of the trench shall be filled in with a mixture of hard and soft material free from boulders larger than 150 mm size. Filling shall be done in layers not exceeding 225 mm thick accompanied by adequate watering, ramming etc. for compacting.

metadata.section: 04.02.04 Method of Quality Control in Welded Joints

children.text: If rocks or other hard objects occur in the back fill along any section of the pipeline, such back fill shall be screened before being placed around the pipe or at the option of the successful suitable backfill from other places shall be transported to and placed around the pipe in such sections.

metadata.section: 04.02.04 Method of Quality Control in Welded Joints

children.text: Settlement of backfill in the trench shall be by means of puddling, tamping or jetting. Poking with metal rods will not be permitted.

metadata.section: 04.02.04 Method of Quality Control in Welded Joints

children.text: Overground Pipelines

metadata.section: 04.02.04 Method of Quality Control in Welded Joints

children.text: Stretch of over ground run of the pipelines shall be suitably supported on RCC pedestals or structural steel columns / trestles. The material and construction of supporting structures such as saddles, anchor blocks, thrust blocks, etc. shall be supplied by the Successful and shall conform to relevant codes and standards.

metadata.section: 04.02.04 Method of Quality Control in Welded Joints

children.text: Saddle supports for overground pipelines shall be either sliding type or fixed type.

metadata.section: 04.02.04 Method of Quality Control in Welded Joints

children.text: Anchor blocks and thrust blocks shall conform to IS:5330 - 1984.

metadata.section: 04.02.04 Method of Quality Control in Welded Joints

children.text: The RCC structures shall be cast at least 3 weeks before the pipelines are laid on them.

metadata.section: 04.02.04 Method of Quality Control in Welded Joints

children.text: Laying of Pipelines

metadata.section: 04.02.04 Method of Quality Control in Welded Joints

children.text: Laying and jointing of welded carbon steel pipes shall generally conform to the guidelines enshrined in IS:5822. The Successful shall mobilize adequate resources, viz. pipe handling equipment, machinery, labour, alignment instruments, etc. for smooth pipe laying operation in scheduled time frame. The Successful shall furnish the list of the aforesaid resources to the Purchaser along with his offer.

metadata.section: 04.02.04 Method of Quality Control in Welded Joints

children.text: The Successful shall make his own arrangement for connecting approaches to transport pipes and specials, hauling equipment, machinery, labour and instruments to the actual site of pipe laying at his own cost.

metadata.section: 04.02.04 Method of Quality Control in Welded Joints

children.text: Inspection of trenches, RCC pedestals / steel structures and internal lining and external protection coating of pipes and specials shall be carried out by the Successful and the Engineer before lowering pipes in position. Care shall be taken to ensure that external protection coating of the pipe is not damaged during lowering and assembly of the pipeline.

metadata.section: 04.02.04 Method of Quality Control in Welded Joints

children.text: The Successful shall follow Purchaser’s erection drawings for laying the pipeline to its final position, alignment and levels.

metadata.section: 04.02.04 Method of Quality Control in Welded Joints

children.text: In order to maintain roundness of pipe sections, steel adjustable type props consisting of minimum six legs shall be fixed inside the pipe wherever necessary. These props shall be fixed vertically and intervals not exceeding 1.8 m. The props shall be kept in position at least for three days after the encasing of the pipe in that position is completed or till backfilling is completed to the full height of backfill over the pipe.

metadata.section: 04.02.04 Method of Quality Control in Welded Joints

children.text: Provision shall be made to safeguard against floatation due to buoyancy by providing sufficient overburden or by providing adequate dead weight, etc.

metadata.section: 04.02.04 Method of Quality Control in Welded Joints

children.text: All openings in the piping shall be kept effectively closed until assembled in the system to avoid foreign matter entering into the system.

metadata.section: 04.02.04 Method of Quality Control in Welded Joints

children.text: All piping shall follow the general arrangement shown in the drawings and shall be cut accurately to measurements established for the work by the Successful and shall be installed without springing.

metadata.section: 04.02.04 Method of Quality Control in Welded Joints

children.text: Flanges of the pipelines, fittings, etc. must be so mounted that top bolt holes are symmetrical about the vertical axis of the flange. When several pipelines are laid in parallel, flanged joints shall be staggered.

metadata.section: 04.02.04 Method of Quality Control in Welded Joints

children.text: Joints should be as far as possible located at one-third span from supports and shall be avoided in the middle of the span. While assembling two consecutive pipelines, care shall be taken to ensure that their longitudinal joints are staggered by 90 degree.

metadata.section: 04.02.04 Method of Quality Control in Welded Joints

children.text: Openings provided in the walls for pipelines shall be closed with bricks or mortar with 10 to 20 mm clearance between the brick work and pipe. The clear space shall be stuffed with felt or asbestos.

metadata.section: 04.02.04 Method of Quality Control in Welded Joints

children.text: The Successful shall supply / fabricate and install pipelines as per design and drawings supplied by the Purchaser / Consultant and shall make provision to completely support all the piping and piping components including but not limited to beam clamps, pipe clamps, fabricated structural supports, guides and anchors, brackets, saddles, U-bolts, etc. Concrete pedestals for supporting the pipes as well as valves where required shall be constructed by the Successful .

metadata.section: 04.02.04 Method of Quality Control in Welded Joints

children.text: The Successful shall supply and erect all specials such as isolation valves, air release valves, drain valves, bends, tees, reducers, stiffener rings, blank flanges, flanges, bolts, nuts, gaskets, etc. as per the erection drawings.

metadata.section: 04.02.04 Method of Quality Control in Welded Joints

children.text: Spacing ofPipesin U/GTunnels&Trenches/OHStructuralTrestles

metadata.section: 04.02.04 Method of Quality Control in Welded Joints

children.text: Pipes on O/H structural trestle.

metadata.section: 04.02.04 Method of Quality Control in Welded Joints

children.text: For O/H trestle, 1m clear walkway with hand railings to be provided. If the width of the trestle is more than 6m, 1m walkway to be provided on both sides of the trestle.For pipe DN 350 & above, Saddle support to be provided. Cleat support can be provided inside pump house. For pipe less than DN 350 U-clamp support to be provided.

metadata.section: 04.02.04 Method of Quality Control in Welded Joints

children.text: The typical cross-section of tunnel/trench showing pipe arrangement is as indicated below:

metadata.section: 04.02.04 Method of Quality Control in Welded Joints

children.text: Pipelines within the shop buildings shall be laid overhead supported form the building columns/side walls/cranes girder etc. wherever this is not possible, the pipelines shallbe laid either in tunnel or in pipe trenches (covered with removable slabs or chequered plates).

metadata.section: 04.02.04 Method of Quality Control in Welded Joints

children.text: Wherever pipelines are crossing form one bay to another parallel bay, they shall be laid in tunnel or supported form the gable end.

metadata.section: 04.02.04 Method of Quality Control in Welded Joints

children.text: Wherever the buried pipelines are crossing the roads and tracks, they shall be suitably encased with mild steel pipes or reinforced concrete casing pipes (NP4 grade) and the different sizes of the encasing pipes shall be as appended:

metadata.section: 04.02.04 Method of Quality Control in Welded Joints

children.text: Erection of Equipment

metadata.section: 04.02.04 Method of Quality Control in Welded Joints

children.text: The foundation slab shall be checked in order to have sufficient clearance between the lower surface of the machine and the foundation slab for the sealing compound and the shims.

metadata.section: 04.02.04 Method of Quality Control in Welded Joints

children.text: The foundation holes for the anchor bolts shall be free and clean and must have been checked for correct positioning in relation to the machine axes as well as for the depth to the elevation points, before erection work is undertaken.

metadata.section: 04.02.04 Method of Quality Control in Welded Joints

children.text: Clean-up, Assembly & Installation

metadata.section: 04.02.04 Method of Quality Control in Welded Joints

children.text: The foundation bases shall be cleaned by water and compressed air to expose the surface.

metadata.section: 04.02.04 Method of Quality Control in Welded Joints

children.text: The top of the concrete foundations shall be thoroughly cleaned with air or air and water-jet prior to placing of equipment on foundations. Concrete surface shall be roughed up by chipping uniformly and the surface shall be washed clean with jet of water to remove fine dust.

metadata.section: 04.02.04 Method of Quality Control in Welded Joints

children.text: The Successful shall place bedplates, cover plates, sole or sub-sole plates on the foundations and shims inserted until the equipment is in level as checked with precision machinist's levels. Required shim stocks, machined steel wedges, if required, shall be furnished by the Successful and used in sufficient numbers to avoid unsupported spans with any perceptible deflection.

metadata.section: 04.02.04 Method of Quality Control in Welded Joints

children.text: Alignment, Levelling& Grouting

metadata.section: 04.02.04 Method of Quality Control in Welded Joints

children.text: The machined base shall be cleaned with a solvent, e.g. kerosene, etc. The equipment shall be placed over the base plate taking care of the foundation bolts. They shall be then aligned with reference to the axes and levelled with the help of shims or any other suitable devices. The lines, levels and alignments of all installed equipment and structural steel work shall be ensured to be within such tolerances as are recommended in the respective equipment manufacturer's instructions.

metadata.section: 04.02.04 Method of Quality Control in Welded Joints

children.text: All required adjustments shall be made by the Successful as directed by the equipment manufacturer. The Successful shall make permanent datum marks on the floors/ permanent structures after installation of all equipment. These marks shall be subject to inspection by the site engineer and are intended to ensure proper alignment and leveling of equipment during re-assembly after maintenance/overhaul, etc.

metadata.section: 04.02.04 Method of Quality Control in Welded Joints

children.text: All motors and driven equipment shall be accurately levelled and aligned to required tolerances. Equipment shipped with motor mounted, shall be rechecked and realigned, if necessary. Alignment, levelling, coupling connections, etc. and final certification for alignments and erection shall be the responsibility of the Successful .

metadata.section: 04.02.04 Method of Quality Control in Welded Joints

children.text: After the equipment/ structure, etc. have been installed, lined, levelled and aligned, the foundation bolts shall be pulled up tightly and the equipment shall be grouted with shrimkomp grout and grouting cured for seven days. When the grout is thoroughly cured, the alignment shall be rechecked. All grouting material including shrimkomp grout shall be supplied by the Successful .

metadata.section: 04.02.04 Method of Quality Control in Welded Joints

children.text: Erection of Valves

metadata.section: 04.02.04 Method of Quality Control in Welded Joints

children.text: Before erection of valves, it shall be ensured that:-

metadata.section: 04.02.04 Method of Quality Control in Welded Joints

children.text: All grit and foreign matter are removed from the inside of the valves

metadata.section: 04.02.04 Method of Quality Control in Welded Joints

children.text: before connecting the pipes.

metadata.section: 04.02.04 Method of Quality Control in Welded Joints

children.text: All the faces are thoroughly cleaned and coated with a thin layer of

metadata.section: 04.02.04 Method of Quality Control in Welded Joints

children.text: mineral grease.

metadata.section: 04.02.04 Method of Quality Control in Welded Joints

children.text: Gate valves shall normally be installed with the spindle vertical except on vertical pipes where the spindle shall be horizontal. On slopes, the sluice valves may preferably be kept vertical if slope is nominal and gradient can be adjusted with the help of pipes on both sides.

metadata.section: 04.02.04 Method of Quality Control in Welded Joints

children.text: The valves should be tightly closed when being installed, to prevent any foreign matter from getting in between the working parts of valves.

metadata.section: 04.02.04 Method of Quality Control in Welded Joints

children.text: It shall be ensured that the joining material sits squarely between the flanges of valves and pipelines without obstructing the waterway.

metadata.section: 04.02.04 Method of Quality Control in Welded Joints

children.text: Site Testing

metadata.section: 04.02.04 Method of Quality Control in Welded Joints

children.text: The Successful shall carry out hydraulic tests on pipeline system in accordance with IS:5822 and guidelines furnished in this document. The Successful shall arrange at his own expenses necessary pumps, other required materials, instruments and consumables to conduct hydraulic tests of the piping system erected by him.

metadata.section: 04.02.04 Method of Quality Control in Welded Joints

children.text: The Successful shall determine the locations of the beginning and the end of each test section. The minimum test pressure shall be 1.5 times the internal maximum working pressure or 10 kg/cm2 which ever is more.

metadata.section: 04.02.04 Method of Quality Control in Welded Joints

children.text: The Successful shall acquire water for hydrostatic tests in a manner satisfactory to the authorities. The quality of water shall be clean without containing silt, suspended matters or harmful corrosive constituents. The Successful shall be responsible for obtaining this water and disposing it of after completion of the test.

metadata.section: 04.02.04 Method of Quality Control in Welded Joints

children.text: All valves and appurtenances shall be checked prior to filling the pipeline to ensure that they are in proper working order and in the proper position for filling. All fittings shall be checked for tightness prior to filling.

metadata.section: 04.02.04 Method of Quality Control in Welded Joints

children.text: Prior to a pressure test, warning signs shall be placed along the way at all public crossings and at all points where there is exposed pipe or appurtenances.

metadata.section: 04.02.04 Method of Quality Control in Welded Joints

children.text: Pressurisation of the test section shall be carried out at a steady rate of not more than 2 bar/minute as follows:

metadata.section: 04.02.04 Method of Quality Control in Welded Joints

children.text: Static head to 75% of the test pressure to be held for half an hour.

metadata.section: 04.02.04 Method of Quality Control in Welded Joints

children.text: Pressurisation to 90% of the test pressure to be held for half an hour.

metadata.section: 04.02.04 Method of Quality Control in Welded Joints

children.text: Pressure to 100% of the test pressure.

metadata.section: 04.02.04 Method of Quality Control in Welded Joints

children.text: Upon completion of pressurisation, the pressure pump shall be shut down and all filling valves closed and all fittings on the test section shall be checked for visible leakage.

metadata.section: 04.02.04 Method of Quality Control in Welded Joints

children.text: Hydraulic test pressure shall be maintained for a minimum duration of 3 (three) hours. At this pressure the pipelines shall be inspected and all joints shall be tapped by a 1.5 kg hand hammer.

metadata.section: 04.02.04 Method of Quality Control in Welded Joints

children.text: Hydraulic test results will be considered satisfactory, if during tests manometric pressure does not decrease and no leakage or mist is found in the joints fittings, etc.

metadata.section: 04.02.04 Method of Quality Control in Welded Joints

children.text: The weld seams of welded pipe shall be inspected full length (100%) for cracks, lack of complete penetration or complete fusion and discontinuities. In addition, the skelp end weld in finished spiral welded pipe shall also be inspected.

metadata.section: 04.02.04 Method of Quality Control in Welded Joints

children.text: If a leak is detected, it shall be located and repaired. If the leak occurs in the pipe seam or the pipe body, the entire faulty joint of the pipe shall be cut out and replaced with a new joint of the pipe. If a leak occurs in the circumferential weld, the pipe shall be cut one pipe diameter on each side of the faulty weld and replaced with a new section of the pipe. Upon completion of the repair, the pipe test section shall be retested in accordance with this specification. The Successful shall be responsible for repair of all failures during hydraulic test.

metadata.section: 04.02.04 Method of Quality Control in Welded Joints

children.text: Site Painting

metadata.section: 04.02.04 Method of Quality Control in Welded Joints

children.text: After erection and testing, all equipment and material that have been finish painted at the manufacturer’s works shall be touched up for any damaged paint work. All items of equipment and material shall be examined for any damage to the paint coat applied at manufacturer’s works and any damaged portion shall be cleaned down to the bare metal, all rust removed and the paint coat made good with identical paint. If necessary, equipment having full painting shall be given one finish coat compatible with shop coats after necessary cleaning and touch-up.

metadata.section: 04.02.04 Method of Quality Control in Welded Joints

children.text: PAINTING SCHEME

metadata.section: 04.02.04 Method of Quality Control in Welded Joints

children.text: This specification covers the materials, tools, facilities and quality requirements for surface preparation and painting of equipment.

metadata.section: 04.02.04 Method of Quality Control in Welded Joints

children.text: The term painting referred herein covers rust preventive, fungus/insects preventive and decorative coating along with surface protection. The complete paint system for any item includes the following basic activities:

metadata.section: 04.02.04 Method of Quality Control in Welded Joints

children.text: Proper surface preparation Application of primer coats

metadata.section: 04.02.04 Method of Quality Control in Welded Joints

children.text: Application of intermediate coats

metadata.section: 04.02.04 Method of Quality Control in Welded Joints

children.text: Application of finished coats

metadata.section: 04.02.04 Method of Quality Control in Welded Joints

children.text: Final paint

metadata.section: 04.02.04 Method of Quality Control in Welded Joints

children.text: All the above coats shall be of quality paint products and of preferred make. The scope of work shall also include supply of paint materials as per specification described herein.

metadata.section: 04.02.04 Method of Quality Control in Welded Joints

children.text: If the successful desires to adopt alternative paint system for any specific item for an improvement or equivalent to the system specified here-in or as per recommendations of paint manufacturer, may do so subject to purchaser’s approval in advance.

metadata.section: 04.02.04 Method of Quality Control in Welded Joints

children.text: Painting Scheme:

metadata.section: 04.02.04 Method of Quality Control in Welded Joints

children.text: Legend

metadata.section: 04.02.04 Method of Quality Control in Welded Joints

children.text: SP - Surface preparation quality as per SIS standard

metadata.section: 04.02.04 Method of Quality Control in Welded Joints

children.text: 2P1- Two (2) coats of Primer paint type P1

metadata.section: 04.02.04 Method of Quality Control in Welded Joints

children.text: 1I 1 - One (1) coats of Intermediate paint type I1

metadata.section: 04.02.04 Method of Quality Control in Welded Joints

children.text: 2F1 - Two (2) coats of Finish paint type F1

metadata.section: 04.02.04 Method of Quality Control in Welded Joints

children.text: DFT - Dry Film Thickness in microns developed

metadata.section: 04.02.04 Method of Quality Control in Welded Joints

children.text: CRT - Clean and Retouch

metadata.section: 04.02.04 Method of Quality Control in Welded Joints

children.text: Surface Preparation

metadata.section: 04.02.04 Method of Quality Control in Welded Joints

children.text: Surface preparation required for paint application shall be such as to clean the surface thoroughly of any material which will be conducive to premature failure of the paint substrates.

metadata.section: 04.02.04 Method of Quality Control in Welded Joints

children.text: The entire surface shall be cleaned of loose substance and foreign materials such as dirt, rust, scale, oil, grease, welding flux etc. in order that the prime coat is rigidly anchored to the virgin metal surface. The surface preparation shall confirm to pictorial representation of surface quality grade of Swedish standards Institution SIS-055900 or equivalent such as SSPC-VIS-1.67 or DIN55928 (part 4) of BS 4232 or IS 1477 (Part 1).

metadata.section: 04.02.04 Method of Quality Control in Welded Joints

children.text: Surface Preparation Grade

metadata.section: 04.02.04 Method of Quality Control in Welded Joints

children.text: Paint Material

metadata.section: 04.02.04 Method of Quality Control in Welded Joints

children.text: Primer Paint

metadata.section: 04.02.04 Method of Quality Control in Welded Joints

children.text: Primer Paint product shall be applied only on dry and clean surfaces.

metadata.section: 04.02.04 Method of Quality Control in Welded Joints

children.text: A single pack air drying phenolic modified alkyd composition with zincphosphate as a primer paint conforming generally to IS:2074.
Air drying time - About 60 minutes (touch dry)
- Over night (hard dry)
Dry film thickness (DFT)/Coat - 40 microns (min)
Temperature resistance - Upto 1000C dry heat
A single pack air drying high build chlorinated rubber based zinc phosphate primer.
Percent chloro rubber - 20 to 22 ( % Chlorine above 65% in chloro rubber)
Air drying time - About 15 minutes (touch dry)
- Over night (hard dry)
DFT/Coat - 50 microns (min)
Temperature resistance - Upto 650C dry heat
A two packair drying Epoxy polyamide resin based redoxide-zinc phosphate primer.
Epoxy content (%wt.) - 15 to 18
Air drying time - About 30 minutes (touchdry)
Overnight (harddry)
DFT/Coat - 30 microns (min)
Temperature resistance - Upto 1200C dry heat
Intermediate Paint
These paints shall be applied over primer coats as an intermediate layer to provide weather proof seal of primer coats.
A single pack high build phenolic based paint with micaceousir on oxide (M10).
Air Drying Time - 4 to 6 hours (touchdry)
- 2 days (hard dry)
DFT/Coat - 75 microns (min)
Temperature resistance - Upto 100 deg C dry heat
Compatible with - Primer P1
A single pack air drying high build chloro based paint with MIO.
Air DryingTime - 15 minutes (touch dry)
- 24 hours (hard dry)
DFT /Coat - 70 microns (min)
Temperature resistance - Upto 65 deg C dry heat
Compatible with - Primer P2, P3 & P4
PVC Coploymer- Resin1:1
Pigments - Micaceous iron oxide (MIO)
DFT/Coat - 80 microns (min)
Temperature resistance - Upto 80 deg C dry heat
Compatible with - Primer P2 & P3
A two pack air drying high builde poxy resin based paint with MIO. Air drying time - 6 to 8 hours (touch dry)
- 7 days (full cure)
DFT/coat - 100 microns
Temperature resistance - Upto1800C dry heat
Compatible with - Primer P4 & P5
Finish Paint
Finish paint costs shall be applied over primer coats and intermediate coats after proper cleaning and touch up primed surface.
A single pack air drying high gloss phenolic alkyd modified synthetic enamel paint suitably pigmented.
A single pack air drying plasticized chloro rubber paint suitably pigmented.
Air drying time - 30 minutes (touch dry)
- 24 hours (hard dry)
DFT/Coat - 35 microns (min)
Temperature resistance - Upto 650C dry heat
Compatible with - Primer P2 & P3, Intermediate I2 & I3
Colour - Nearly all shades except few.
APVC – Copolymeralkyd based enamel.
Density - 1.17U+U0.05
Total solids (1wt) - 55U+U2
DFT/Coat - 40 microns
Compatible with - P2 and P3
A two pack air drying epoxy polyamide enamel suitably pigmented.
Air drying time - 2 to 3 hours (touchdry)
DFT/Coat - 7 days (full cure)
Temperature resistance -
Compatible with - Upto 1300C dry heat
Colour - Primer P4 & P5,
Generally all shades
Painting of structures
All fabricated steel structures shall have a minimum of two coats of primer paint before despatch to site.

Parts of steel structures embedded in concrete shall be given a protective coat of Portland cement slurry immediately after fabrication and after surfaces of this part is thoroughly cleaned from grease, rust, mill scales, etc. No paint shall be applied on this part.
All structures shall receive appropriate number of primer and finishing coats in order to achieve overall DFT as per design drawings/specification.
Paint Application
Paint shall be applied in accordance with manufacturer’s recommendations as supplemented by this specification. The work shall generally be carried out as per !S 1477 (Part II).

Paint shall not be applied in rain, wind, fog or at relative humidity of 80% and above or when the surface temperature is below dew point resulting in condensation of moisture. Paint shall generally not be applied when the ambient temperature is 50 C and below.
Each coat of paint shall be continuous, free of pores and of even film thickness without thin spots.
Each coat of paint shall be allowed to dry sufficiently before application of the next coat to avoid damage such as lifting or loss of adhesion.
Colour Code

Shades of finish coat to be applied shall be as per IS 5 and of wide acceptability in water supply works. Prior approval shall be taken from the Purchaser after placement of order before adopting finalcolour shade for any particular item being painted.

The Successful shall furnish paint manufacturer’s technical data sheet pertaining to the paint selected. The data sheet shall inter alia indicate the relevant standards, if any, composition in weight percentage of pigments, vehicles, additives, drying time, viscosity, spreading rate, flash point, method of application, quality of surface preparation required, corrosion resistance properties and colour shades available.
Flushing of Pipelines

Various types of control valves, cocks, orifice plates, filters, Magnetic flow meters etc. shall be removed and fitted with distance pieces. The above fittings will be flushed and cleaned manually. All pumps, strainers, etc. shall be isolated during flushing and cleaning operation.

All the pumps and equipment in the cleaning circuit shall be isolated by blanking and by passing through temporary connections on the pipelines. If filters and strainers are kept in the main circuit during flushing operation, these will be removed, cleaned and refitted after flushing is completed.
The pipes not coming in direct circulation path shall be dismantled and flushed separately by making suitable closed ring.
Trial Run and Commissioning

Since it is a new system, it is quite possible that the pipeline may be fouled with cement, deposits, grease, oil and other suspended matters. As such, entire pipeline shall be thoroughly flushed for removal of foreign materials from the system. Flushing shall be continued till the system is completely cleaned. During flushing, valves and appurtenances shall be isolated and bypassed where necessary.
Before preparation for commissioning, all the equipment and pipeline shall be certified by the Employer for commissioning.

Preliminary acceptance of the work shall be conducted unitwise or groupwise after the completion of work as per a mutually agreed upon schedule. Final acceptance of the system as a whole shall take place after the complete facilities have been commissioned and satisfactorily operated over a period mutually agreed upon.

The plant and equipment shall be considered commissioned only after the successful performance guarantee tests. The warranty clause will be operative only after the completion of successful guaranteed tests.
**** End of Clause***
General

metadata.section: 13 TECHNICAL SPECIFICATION FOR ERECTION WORKS FOR FUEL GAS & GASEOUS UTILITY PIPING SYSTEM

children.text: The fuel gas and gaseous utility facilities covered herein includes distribution of piping system for Mixed gas, LPG/Propane, Compressed air, Instrument air, Steam, Oxygen and Nitrogen.

metadata.section: 13 TECHNICAL SPECIFICATION FOR ERECTION WORKS FOR FUEL GAS & GASEOUS UTILITY PIPING SYSTEM

children.text: All the above gas & utilities shall be made available by the Purchaser at specified parameters at specified battery limits and shall distribute the same to the various consuming points as per the scope of work as described herein under different clauses.

metadata.section: 13 TECHNICAL SPECIFICATION FOR ERECTION WORKS FOR FUEL GAS & GASEOUS UTILITY PIPING SYSTEM

children.text: Scope of work

metadata.section: 13 TECHNICAL SPECIFICATION FOR ERECTION WORKS FOR FUEL GAS & GASEOUS UTILITY PIPING SYSTEM

children.text: The scope of work shall be inline with Chapter No. 3. Of this TS.

metadata.section: 13 TECHNICAL SPECIFICATION FOR ERECTION WORKS FOR FUEL GAS & GASEOUS UTILITY PIPING SYSTEM

children.text: It will broadly covers taking over & handling of materials, fabrication, erection, flushing, testing and commissioning for fuel gas and gaseous utility piping system. All material for piping system will be supplied by the Purchaser’s other agency (Pkg. HSM-1).

metadata.section: 13 TECHNICAL SPECIFICATION FOR ERECTION WORKS FOR FUEL GAS & GASEOUS UTILITY PIPING SYSTEM

children.text: Erection shall be done based on the engineering drawings of the Purchaser’s other agency (Pkg. HSM-1).

metadata.section: 13 TECHNICAL SPECIFICATION FOR ERECTION WORKS FOR FUEL GAS & GASEOUS UTILITY PIPING SYSTEM

children.text: The shall be responsible for complete readiness of the erected system for fuel gas and gaseous utilities in line with engineering drawings for successful commissioning of the system.

metadata.section: 13 TECHNICAL SPECIFICATION FOR ERECTION WORKS FOR FUEL GAS & GASEOUS UTILITY PIPING SYSTEM

children.text: For piping systems and associated equipment from Take Over Points to various consumers, shall be responsible for erection of pipe supporting structures, platforms, stairs etc. and construction of its foundations as per structural/ civil/ other engineering drawings to be provided by Purchaser/ Consultant.

metadata.section: 13 TECHNICAL SPECIFICATION FOR ERECTION WORKS FOR FUEL GAS & GASEOUS UTILITY PIPING SYSTEM

metadata.section: 14 PRELIMINARY ACCEPTANCE TEST, COMMISSIONING & FINAL ACCEPTANCE

children.text: After completion of erection of the plant and equipment of Reheating Furnaces, Hot Strip Mill, Cut To length and Cranes along withutilities and auxiliaries by the under supervision of equipment supplier, the tests for Preliminary Acceptanceof the units shall be carried out. PAT shall be conducted by the Supplier of the Equipment under his so responsibility and employing supplier’s manpower. Necessary utilities will be provided by the Employer during the PAT.
Preliminary Acceptance Test for Erection Works shall be considered as successful on issuance of PAC for HSM1 (Process Technology Package) and HSM3 (Crane Package).

Preliminary Acceptance Tests, trials for individual equipment / sub-assemblies / sub-units shall be carried out. Trial run shall comprise idle, no-load, part load and full load tests, as applicable, and shall be carried out by the equipment supplier. Necessary manpower for assistance during tests, trials to be provided by the .
Integrated trial run shall also be carried out including auxiliaries & services.
Detail programme of cold tests shall be informed to the in advance of the cold tests.
Results of cold tests shall be recorded jointly by the Successful , equipment supplier and Purchaser.
shall liquidate the defects related to his scope of work of and / or deficiencies indicated / listed by the Employer / Consultant / Supplier during trial runs.

On successful completion of the Preliminary Acceptance Tests (PAT) and liquidation of all the necessary defects required to start the hot trial and commissioning, the hot trial with charging of cold slab to reheating furnace shall be carried out. Commissioning shall be conducted by the Supplier of the Equipment under his so responsibility and Employer will provide necessary raw material, consumables and utilities during the commissioning.

After successful completion of hot trials when all the equipment, systems and sub systems are found to be working satisfactorily and functioning, the equipment supplier shall carry out commissioning test of the plant. shall deploy necessary resources during commissioning test.
Commissioning for Erection Works shall be considered as successful commissioning for HSM1 (Process Technology Package).
Defects related to the scope of work of the observed during hot trial and commissioning test shall be rectified by the at his own cost.

The Contractor shall make good all defects deficiencies and omissions noted down during trial run and commissioning and shall inform in advance the Purchaser/ his representative for conducting inspection for liquidated defects. Final acceptance certificate will be issued by the Purchaser / his representative only after all defects / deficiencies / omissions noted have been rectified.
**End of Clauses**
